1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # Post processing that we can do after a post has already been cooked.
4: # For example, inserting the onebox content, or image sizes/thumbnails.
5: 
6: class CookedPostProcessor
7:   include CookedProcessorMixin
8: 
9:   attr_reader :cooking_options, :doc
10: 
11:   def initialize(post, opts = {})
12:     @dirty = false
13:     @opts = opts
14:     @post = post
15:     @model = post
16:     @previous_cooked = (@post.cooked || "").dup
17:     # NOTE: we re-cook the post here in order to prevent timing issues with edits
18:     # cf. https://meta.discourse.org/t/edit-of-rebaked-post-doesnt-show-in-html-only-in-raw/33815/6
19:     @cooking_options = post.cooking_options || opts[:cooking_options] || {}
20:     @cooking_options[:topic_id] = post.topic_id
21:     @cooking_options = @cooking_options.symbolize_keys
22:     @should_secure_uploads = @post.should_secure_uploads?
23:     @category_id = @post&.topic&.category_id
24: 
25:     cooked = post.cook(post.raw, @cooking_options)
26:     @doc = Loofah.html5_fragment(cooked)
27:     @has_oneboxes = post.post_analyzer.found_oneboxes?
28:     @size_cache = {}
29: 
30:     @disable_dominant_color = !!opts[:disable_dominant_color]
31:     @omit_nofollow = post.omit_nofollow?
32:   end
33: 
34:   def post_process(new_post: false)
35:     DistributedMutex.synchronize("post_process_#{@post.id}", validity: 10.minutes) do
36:       DiscourseEvent.trigger(:before_post_process_cooked, @doc, @post)
37:       update_uploads_secure_status
38:       remove_full_quote_on_direct_reply if new_post
39:       post_process_oneboxes
40:       post_process_images
41:       post_process_videos
42:       add_blocked_hotlinked_media_placeholders
43:       post_process_quotes
44:       optimize_urls
45:       remove_user_ids
46:       update_post_image
47:       enforce_nofollow
48:       grant_badges
49:       @post.link_post_uploads(fragments: @doc)
50:       DiscourseEvent.trigger(:post_process_cooked, @doc, @post)
51:       nil
52:     end
53:   end
54: 
55:   def has_emoji?
56:     (@doc.css("img.emoji") - @doc.css(".quote img")).size > 0
57:   end
58: 
59:   def grant_badges
60:     return if @post.user.blank? || !Guardian.new.can_see?(@post)
61: 
62:     BadgeGranter.grant(Badge.find(Badge::FirstEmoji), @post.user, post_id: @post.id) if has_emoji?
63:     if @has_oneboxes
64:       BadgeGranter.grant(Badge.find(Badge::FirstOnebox), @post.user, post_id: @post.id)
65:     end
66:     if @post.is_reply_by_email?
67:       BadgeGranter.grant(Badge.find(Badge::FirstReplyByEmail), @post.user, post_id: @post.id)
68:     end
69:   end
70: 
71:   def post_process_quotes
72:     @doc
73:       .css("aside.quote")
74:       .each do |q|
75:         post_number = q["data-post"]
76:         topic_id = q["data-topic"]
77:         if topic_id && post_number
78:           comparer = QuoteComparer.new(topic_id.to_i, post_number.to_i, q.css("blockquote").text)
79: 
80:           q["class"] = ((q["class"] || "") + " quote-post-not-found").strip if comparer.missing?
81:           q["class"] = ((q["class"] || "") + " quote-modified").strip if comparer.modified?
82:         end
83:       end
84:   end
85: 
86:   def update_uploads_secure_status
87:     @post.update_uploads_secure_status(source: "post processor")
88:   end
89: 
90:   def remove_full_quote_on_direct_reply
91:     return if !SiteSetting.remove_full_quote
92:     return if @post.post_number == 1
93:     return if @doc.xpath("aside[contains(@class, 'quote')]").size != 1
94: 
95:     previous =
96:       Post
97:         .where(
98:           "post_number < ? AND topic_id = ? AND post_type = ? AND NOT hidden",
99:           @post.post_number,
100:           @post.topic_id,
101:           Post.types[:regular],
102:         )
103:         .order("post_number DESC")
104:         .limit(1)
105:         .pluck(:cooked)
106:         .first
107: 
108:     return if previous.blank?
109: 
110:     previous_text = Nokogiri::HTML5.fragment(previous).text.strip
111:     quoted_text = @doc.css("aside.quote:first-child blockquote").first&.text&.strip || ""
112: 
113:     return if previous_text.gsub(/(\s){2,}/, '\1') != quoted_text.gsub(/(\s){2,}/, '\1')
114: 
115:     quote_regexp = %r{\A\s*\[quote.+\[/quote\]}im
116:     quoteless_raw = @post.raw.sub(quote_regexp, "").strip
117: 
118:     return if @post.raw.strip == quoteless_raw
119: 
120:     PostRevisor.new(@post).revise!(
121:       Discourse.system_user,
122:       { raw: quoteless_raw, edit_reason: I18n.t(:removed_direct_reply_full_quotes) },
123:       skip_validations: true,
124:       bypass_bump: true,
125:     )
126:   end
127: 
128:   def extract_images_for_post
129:     # all images with a src attribute
130:     @doc.css("img[src]") -
131:       # minus emojis
132:       @doc.css("img.emoji") -
133:       # minus images inside quotes
134:       @doc.css(".quote img") -
135:       # minus onebox site icons
136:       @doc.css("img.site-icon") -
137:       # minus onebox avatars
138:       @doc.css("img.onebox-avatar") - @doc.css("img.onebox-avatar-inline") -
139:       # minus github onebox profile images
140:       @doc.css(".onebox.githubfolder img")
141:   end
142: 
143:   def update_post_image
144:     upload = nil
145:     images = extract_images_for_post
146: 
147:     @post.each_upload_url(fragments: images.css("[data-thumbnail]")) do |url, path, sha1|
148:       upload = Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)
149:       break if upload
150:     end
151: 
152:     if upload.nil? # No specified thumbnail. Use any image:
153:       @post.each_upload_url(fragments: images.css(":not([data-thumbnail])")) do |url, path, sha1|
154:         upload = Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)
155:         break if upload
156:       end
157:     end
158: 
159:     if upload.present?
160:       @post.update_column(:image_upload_id, upload.id) # post
161:       if @post.is_first_post? # topic
162:         @post.topic.update_column(:image_upload_id, upload.id)
163:         extra_sizes =
164:           ThemeModifierHelper.new(theme_ids: Theme.user_selectable.pluck(:id)).topic_thumbnail_sizes
165:         @post.topic.generate_thumbnails!(extra_sizes: extra_sizes)
166:       end
167:     else
168:       @post.update_column(:image_upload_id, nil) if @post.image_upload_id
169:       if @post.topic.image_upload_id && @post.is_first_post?
170:         @post.topic.update_column(:image_upload_id, nil)
171:       end
172:       nil
173:     end
174:   end
175: 
176:   def optimize_urls
177:     %w[href data-download-href].each do |selector|
178:       @doc.css("a[#{selector}]").each { |a| a[selector] = UrlHelper.cook_url(a[selector].to_s) }
179:     end
180: 
181:     %w[src].each do |selector|
182:       @doc
183:         .css("img[#{selector}], video[#{selector}]")
184:         .each do |img|
185:           custom_emoji = img["class"]&.include?("emoji-custom") && Emoji.custom?(img["title"])
186:           img[selector] = UrlHelper.cook_url(
187:             img[selector].to_s,
188:             secure: @should_secure_uploads && !custom_emoji,
189:           )
190:         end
191:     end
192:   end
193: 
194:   def remove_user_ids
195:     @doc
196:       .css("a[href]")
197:       .each do |a|
198:         uri =
199:           begin
200:             URI(a["href"])
201:           rescue URI::Error
202:             next
203:           end
204:         next if uri.hostname != Discourse.current_hostname
205: 
206:         query = Rack::Utils.parse_nested_query(uri.query)
207:         next if !query.delete("u")
208: 
209:         uri.query = query.map { |k, v| "#{k}=#{v}" }.join("&").presence
210:         a["href"] = uri.to_s
211:       end
212:   end
213: 
214:   def enforce_nofollow
215:     add_nofollow = !@omit_nofollow && SiteSetting.add_rel_nofollow_to_user_content
216:     PrettyText.add_rel_attributes_to_user_content(@doc, add_nofollow)
217:   end
218: 
219:   private
220: 
221:   def post_process_videos
222:     changes_made = false
223: 
224:     begin
225:       @doc
226:         .css(".video-placeholder-container")
227:         .each do |container|
228:           src = container["data-video-src"]
229:           next if src.blank?
230: 
231:           # Look for optimized video
232:           upload = Upload.get_from_url(src)
233:           if upload && optimized_video = OptimizedVideo.find_by(upload_id: upload.id)
234:             optimized_url = Discourse.store.cdn_url(optimized_video.optimized_upload.url)
235:             # Only update if the URL is different
236:             if container["data-video-src"] != optimized_url
237:               container["data-original-video-src"] = container["data-video-src"] unless container[
238:                 "data-original-video-src"
239:               ]
240:               container["data-video-src"] = optimized_url
241:               changes_made = true
242:             end
243:             # Ensure we maintain reference to original upload
244:             @post.link_post_uploads(fragments: @doc)
245:           end
246:         end
247: 
248:       # Update the post's cooked content if changes were made
249:       if changes_made
250:         new_cooked = @doc.to_html
251:         @post.cooked = new_cooked
252:         if !@post.save
253:           Rails.logger.error("Failed to save post: #{@post.errors.full_messages.join(", ")}")
254:         end
255:       end
256:     rescue => e
257:       Rails.logger.error("Error in post_process_videos: #{e.message}")
258:       Rails.logger.error(e.backtrace.join("\n"))
259:       raise
260:     end
261:   end
262: 
263:   def add_blocked_hotlinked_media_placeholders
264:     @doc
265:       .css(
266:         [
267:           "[#{PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR}]",
268:           "[#{PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRCSET_ATTR}]",
269:         ].join(","),
270:       )
271:       .each do |el|
272:         src =
273:           el[PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR] ||
274:             el[PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRCSET_ATTR]&.split(",")&.first&.split(" ")&.first
275: 
276:         if el.name == "img"
277:           add_blocked_hotlinked_image_placeholder!(el)
278:           next
279:         end
280: 
281:         el = el.parent if %w[video audio].include?(el.parent.name)
282: 
283:         el = el.parent if el.parent.classes.include?("video-container")
284: 
285:         add_blocked_hotlinked_media_placeholder!(el, src)
286:       end
287:   end
288: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module CookedProcessorMixin
4:   GIF_SOURCES_REGEXP = %r{(giphy|tenor)\.com/}
5:   LIGHTBOX_WRAPPER_CSS_CLASS = "lightbox-wrapper"
6:   MIN_LIGHTBOX_WIDTH = 100
7:   MIN_LIGHTBOX_HEIGHT = 100
8: 
9:   def post_process_oneboxes
10:     limit = SiteSetting.max_oneboxes_per_post - @doc.css("aside.onebox, a.inline-onebox").size
11:     oneboxes = {}
12:     inlineOneboxes = {}
13: 
14:     Oneboxer.apply(@doc, extra_paths: [".inline-onebox-loading"]) do |url, element|
15:       is_onebox = element["class"] == Oneboxer::ONEBOX_CSS_CLASS
16:       map = is_onebox ? oneboxes : inlineOneboxes
17:       skip_onebox = limit <= 0 && !map[url]
18: 
19:       if skip_onebox
20:         if is_onebox
21:           element.remove_class("onebox")
22:         else
23:           remove_inline_onebox_loading_class(element)
24:         end
25: 
26:         next
27:       end
28: 
29:       limit -= 1
30:       map[url] = true
31: 
32:       if is_onebox
33:         onebox =
34:           Oneboxer.onebox(
35:             url,
36:             invalidate_oneboxes: !!@opts[:invalidate_oneboxes],
37:             user_id: @model&.user_id,
38:             category_id: @category_id,
39:           )
40: 
41:         @has_oneboxes = true if onebox.present?
42:         onebox
43:       else
44:         process_inline_onebox(element)
45:         false
46:       end
47:     end
48: 
49:     PrettyText.sanitize_hotlinked_media(@doc)
50: 
51:     oneboxed_images.each do |img|
52:       next if img["src"].blank?
53: 
54:       parent = img.parent
55: 
56:       if respond_to?(:process_hotlinked_image, true)
57:         still_an_image = process_hotlinked_image(img)
58:         next if !still_an_image
59:       end
60: 
61:       # make sure we grab dimensions for oneboxed images
62:       # and wrap in a div
63:       limit_size!(img)
64: 
65:       next if img["class"]&.include?("onebox-avatar")
66: 
67:       parent = parent&.parent if parent&.name == "a"
68:       parent_class = parent && parent["class"]
69:       width = img["width"].to_i
70:       height = img["height"].to_i
71: 
72:       if parent_class&.include?("onebox-body") && width > 0 && height > 0
73:         # special instruction for width == height, assume we are dealing with an avatar
74:         if (img["width"].to_i == img["height"].to_i)
75:           found = false
76:           parent = img
77:           while parent = parent.parent
78:             if parent["class"] && parent["class"].match?(/\b(allowlistedgeneric|discoursetopic)\b/)
79:               found = true
80:               break
81:             end
82:           end
83: 
84:           if found
85:             img["class"] = img["class"].to_s + " onebox-avatar"
86:             next
87:           end
88:         end
89: 
90:         if width < 64 && height < 64
91:           img["class"] = img["class"].to_s + " onebox-full-image"
92:         else
93:           img.delete("width")
94:           img.delete("height")
95:           new_parent =
96:             img.add_next_sibling(
97:               "<div class='aspect-image' style='--aspect-ratio:#{width}/#{height};'/>",
98:             )
99:           new_parent.first.add_child(img)
100:         end
101:       elsif (
102:             parent_class&.include?("instagram-images") || parent_class&.include?("tweet-images") ||
103:               parent_class&.include?("scale-images")
104:           ) && width > 0 && height > 0
105:         img.remove_attribute("width")
106:         img.remove_attribute("height")
107:         parent["class"] = "aspect-image-full-size"
108:         parent["style"] = "--aspect-ratio:#{width}/#{height};"
109:       end
110:     end
111: 
112:     if @omit_nofollow || !SiteSetting.add_rel_nofollow_to_user_content
113:       @doc
114:         .css(".onebox-body a[rel], .onebox a[rel]")
115:         .each do |a|
116:           rel_values = a["rel"].split(" ").map(&:downcase)
117:           rel_values.delete("nofollow")
118:           rel_values.delete("ugc")
119:           if rel_values.blank?
120:             a.remove_attribute("rel")
121:           else
122:             a["rel"] = rel_values.join(" ")
123:           end
124:         end
125:     end
126:   end
127: 
128:   def post_process_images
129:     extract_images.each do |img|
130:       still_an_image = process_hotlinked_image(img)
131:       convert_to_link!(img) if still_an_image
132:     end
133:   end
134: 
135:   def extract_images
136:     # all images with a src attribute
137:     @doc.css("img[src], img[#{PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR}]") -
138:       # minus data images
139:       @doc.css("img[src^='data']") -
140:       # minus emojis
141:       @doc.css("img.emoji")
142:   end
143: 
144:   def limit_size!(img)
145:     # retrieve the size from
146:     #  1) the width/height attributes
147:     #  2) the dimension from the preview (image_sizes)
148:     #  3) the dimension of the original image (HTTP request)
149:     w, h =
150:       get_size_from_attributes(img) || get_size_from_image_sizes(img["src"], @opts[:image_sizes]) ||
151:         get_size(img["src"])
152: 
153:     # limit the size of the thumbnail
154:     img["width"], img["height"] = ImageSizer.resize(w, h)
155:   end
156: 
157:   def get_size_from_attributes(img)
158:     w, h = img["width"].to_i, img["height"].to_i
159:     return w, h if w > 0 && h > 0
160:     # if only width or height are specified attempt to scale image
161:     if w > 0 || h > 0
162:       w = w.to_f
163:       h = h.to_f
164: 
165:       return unless original_image_size = get_size(img["src"])
166:       original_width, original_height = original_image_size.map(&:to_f)
167: 
168:       if w > 0
169:         ratio = w / original_width
170:         [w.floor, (original_height * ratio).floor]
171:       else
172:         ratio = h / original_height
173:         [(original_width * ratio).floor, h.floor]
174:       end
175:     end
176:   end
177: 
178:   def get_size_from_image_sizes(src, image_sizes)
179:     return if image_sizes.blank?
180:     image_sizes.each do |image_size|
181:       url, size = image_size[0], image_size[1]
182:       if url && src && url.include?(src) && size && size["width"].to_i > 0 &&
183:            size["height"].to_i > 0
184:         return size["width"], size["height"]
185:       end
186:     end
187:     nil
188:   end
189: 
190:   def add_to_size_cache(url, w, h)
191:     @size_cache[url] = [w, h]
192:   end
193: 
194:   def get_size(url)
195:     return @size_cache[url] if @size_cache.has_key?(url)
196: 
197:     absolute_url = url
198:     absolute_url = Discourse.base_url_no_prefix + absolute_url if absolute_url =~ %r{\A/[^/]}
199: 
200:     return unless absolute_url
201: 
202:     # FastImage fails when there's no scheme
203:     absolute_url = SiteSetting.scheme + ":" + absolute_url if absolute_url.start_with?("//")
204: 
205:     # we can't direct FastImage to our secure-uploads url because it bounces
206:     # anonymous requests with a 404 error
207:     if url && Upload.secure_uploads_url?(url)
208:       absolute_url =
209:         Upload.signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url(absolute_url, include_content_disposition: false)
210:     end
211: 
212:     return unless is_valid_image_url?(absolute_url)
213: 
214:     upload = Upload.get_from_url(absolute_url)
215:     if upload && upload.width && upload.width > 0
216:       @size_cache[url] = [upload.width, upload.height]
217:     else
218:       @size_cache[url] = FastImage.size(absolute_url)
219:     end
220:   rescue Zlib::BufError, URI::Error, OpenSSL::SSL::SSLError
221:     # FastImage.size raises BufError for some gifs, leave it.
222:   end
223: 
224:   def get_filename(upload, src)
225:     return File.basename(src) unless upload
226:     return upload.original_filename unless upload.original_filename =~ /\Ablob(\.png)?\z/i
227:     I18n.t("upload.pasted_image_filename")
228:   end
229: 
230:   def is_valid_image_url?(url)
231:     uri = URI.parse(url)
232:     %w[http https].include? uri.scheme
233:   rescue URI::Error
234:   end
235: 
236:   def add_large_image_placeholder!(img)
237:     url = img["src"]
238: 
239:     is_hyperlinked = is_a_hyperlink?(img)
240: 
241:     placeholder = create_node("div", "large-image-placeholder")
242:     img.add_next_sibling(placeholder)
243:     placeholder.add_child(img)
244: 
245:     a = create_link_node(nil, url, true)
246:     img.add_next_sibling(a)
247: 
248:     span = create_span_node("url", url)
249:     a.add_child(span)
250:     span.add_previous_sibling(create_icon_node("far-image"))

403:   def is_svg?(img)
404:     path =
405:       begin
406:         URI(img["src"]).path
407:       rescue URI::Error
408:         nil
409:       end
410: 
411:     File.extname(path) == ".svg" if path

414:   def convert_to_link!(img)
415:     w, h = img["width"].to_i, img["height"].to_i
416:     user_width, user_height =
417:       (w > 0 && h > 0 && [w, h]) || get_size_from_attributes(img) ||
418:         get_size_from_image_sizes(img["src"], @opts[:image_sizes])
419: 
420:     limit_size!(img)
421: 
422:     src = img["src"]
423:     return if src.blank? || is_a_hyperlink?(img)
424: 
425:     upload = Upload.get_from_url(src)
426: 
427:     original_width, original_height = nil
428: 
429:     if (upload.present?)
430:       original_width = upload.width || 0
431:       original_height = upload.height || 0
432:     else
433:       original_width, original_height = (get_size(src) || [0, 0]).map(&:to_i)
434:       if original_width == 0 || original_height == 0
435:         Rails.logger.info "Can't reach '#{src}' to get its dimension."
436:         return
437:       end
438:     end
439: 
440:     if (upload.present? && upload.animated?) || src.match?(GIF_SOURCES_REGEXP)
441:       img.add_class("animated")
442:     end
443: 
444:     generate_thumbnail =
445:       original_width > SiteSetting.max_image_width || original_height > SiteSetting.max_image_height
446: 
447:     user_width, user_height = [original_width, original_height] if user_width.to_i <= 0 &&
448:       user_height.to_i <= 0
449:     width, height = user_width, user_height
450: 
451:     crop =
452:       SiteSetting.min_ratio_to_crop > 0 && width.to_f / height.to_f < SiteSetting.min_ratio_to_crop
453: 
454:     if crop
455:       width, height = ImageSizer.crop(width, height)
456:       img["width"], img["height"] = width, height
457:     else
458:       width, height = ImageSizer.resize(width, height)
459:     end
460: 
461:     if upload.present?
462:       if generate_thumbnail
463:         upload.create_thumbnail!(width, height, crop: crop)
464: 
465:         each_responsive_ratio do |ratio|
466:           resized_w = (width * ratio).to_i
467:           resized_h = (height * ratio).to_i
468: 
469:           if upload.width && resized_w <= upload.width
470:             upload.create_thumbnail!(resized_w, resized_h, crop: crop)
471:           end
472:         end
473:       end
474: 
475:       return if upload.animated?
476: 
477:       if img.ancestors(".onebox, .onebox-body").blank? && !img.classes.include?("onebox")
478:         add_lightbox!(img, original_width, original_height, upload, crop)
479:       end
480: 
481:       optimize_image!(img, upload, cropped: crop) if generate_thumbnail
482:     end
483:   end

485:   def process_hotlinked_image(img)
486:     onebox = img.ancestors(".onebox, .onebox-body").first
487: 
488:     # Skip hotlinked media processing if @post is not available (e.g., for chat messages)
489:     return true if @post.nil?
490: 
491:     @hotlinked_map ||= @post.post_hotlinked_media.preload(:upload).index_by(&:url)
492:     normalized_src =
493:       PostHotlinkedMedia.normalize_src(img["src"] || img[PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR])
494:     info = @hotlinked_map[normalized_src]
495: 
496:     still_an_image = true
497: 
498:     if info&.too_large?
499:       if !onebox || onebox.element_children.size == 1
500:         add_large_image_placeholder!(img)
501:       else
502:         img.remove
503:       end
504: 
505:       still_an_image = false
506:     elsif info&.download_failed?
507:       if !onebox || onebox.element_children.size == 1
508:         add_broken_image_placeholder!(img)
509:       else
510:         img.remove
511:       end
512: 
513:       still_an_image = false
514:     elsif info&.downloaded? && upload = info&.upload
515:       img["src"] = UrlHelper.cook_url(upload.url, secure: @should_secure_uploads)
516:       img["data-dominant-color"] = upload.dominant_color(calculate_if_missing: true).presence
517:       img.delete(PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR)
518:     end
519: 
520:     still_an_image
521:   end
522: 
523:   def optimize_image!(img, upload, cropped: false)
524:     w, h = img["width"].to_i, img["height"].to_i
525:     onebox = img.ancestors(".onebox, .onebox-body").first
526: 
527:     # note: optimize_urls cooks the src further after this
528:     thumbnail = upload.thumbnail(w, h)
529:     if thumbnail && thumbnail.filesize.to_i < upload.filesize
530:       img["src"] = thumbnail.url
531: 
532:       srcset = +""
533: 
534:       # Skip srcset for onebox images. Because onebox thumbnails by default
535:       # are fairly small the width/height of the smallest thumbnail is likely larger
536:       # than what the onebox thumbnail size will be displayed at, so we shouldn't
537:       # need to upscale for retina devices
538:       if !onebox
539:         each_responsive_ratio do |ratio|
540:           resized_w = (w * ratio).to_i
541:           resized_h = (h * ratio).to_i
542: 
543:           if !cropped && upload.width && resized_w > upload.width
544:             cooked_url = UrlHelper.cook_url(upload.url, secure: @should_secure_uploads)
545:             srcset << ", #{cooked_url} #{ratio.to_s.sub(/\.0\z/, "")}x"
546:           elsif t = upload.thumbnail(resized_w, resized_h)
547:             cooked_url = UrlHelper.cook_url(t.url, secure: @should_secure_uploads)
548:             srcset << ", #{cooked_url} #{ratio.to_s.sub(/\.0\z/, "")}x"
549:           end
550: 
551:           img[
552:             "srcset"
553:           ] = "#{UrlHelper.cook_url(img["src"], secure: @should_secure_uploads)}#{srcset}" if srcset.present?
554:         end
555:       end
556:     else
557:       img["src"] = upload.url
558:     end
559: 
560:     if !@disable_dominant_color &&
561:          (color = upload.dominant_color(calculate_if_missing: true).presence)
562:       img["data-dominant-color"] = color
563:     end
564:   end
565: 
566:   def add_lightbox!(img, original_width, original_height, upload, crop)
567:     return if original_width < MIN_LIGHTBOX_WIDTH || original_height < MIN_LIGHTBOX_HEIGHT
568: 
569:     # first, create a div to hold our lightbox
570:     lightbox = create_node("div", LIGHTBOX_WRAPPER_CSS_CLASS)
571:     img.add_next_sibling(lightbox)
572:     lightbox.add_child(img)
573: 
574:     # then, the link to our larger image
575:     src_url = Upload.secure_uploads_url?(img["src"]) ? upload&.url || img["src"] : img["src"]
576:     src = UrlHelper.cook_url(src_url, secure: @should_secure_uploads)
577: 
578:     a = create_link_node("lightbox", src)
579:     img.add_next_sibling(a)
580: 
581:     a["data-download-href"] = Discourse.store.download_url(upload) if upload
582: 
583:     a.add_child(img)
584: 
585:     # then, some overlay informations
586:     meta = create_node("div", "meta")
587:     img.add_next_sibling(meta)
588: 
589:     filename = get_filename(upload, img["src"])
590:     informations = +"#{original_width}×#{original_height}"
591:     informations << " #{upload.human_filesize}" if upload
592: 
593:     a["title"] = img["title"] || img["alt"] || filename
594:

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module PrettyText
4:   module Helpers
5:     extend self
6: 
7:     TAG_HASHTAG_POSTFIX = "::tag"
8: 
9:     # functions here are available to v8
10:     def t(key, opts)
11:       key = "js." + key
12:       return I18n.t(key) if opts.blank?
13:       str = I18n.t(key, Hash[opts.entries].symbolize_keys).dup
14:       opts.each { |k, v| str.gsub!("{{#{k}}}", v.to_s) }
15:       str
16:     end
17: 
18:     def avatar_template(username)
19:       return "" unless username
20:       user = User.find_by(username_lower: username.downcase)
21:       return "" if user.blank?
22: 
23:       # TODO: Add support for ES6 and call `avatar-template` directly
24:       UrlHelper.schemaless(UrlHelper.absolute(user.avatar_template))
25:     end
26: 
27:     def lookup_primary_user_group(username)
28:       return "" unless username
29:       user = User.find_by(username_lower: username.downcase)
30:       return "" if user.blank?
31: 
32:       user.primary_group.try(:name) || ""
33:     end
34: 
35:     # Overwrite this in a plugin to change how markdown can format
36:     # usernames on the server side
37:     def format_username(username)
38:       username
39:     end
40: 
41:     def lookup_upload_urls(urls)
42:       map = {}
43:       result = {}
44: 
45:       urls.each do |url|
46:         sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_short_url(url)
47:         if (url.split(".")[1].nil?) # video sha1 without extension for thumbnail
48:           thumbnail = Upload.where("original_filename LIKE ?", "#{sha1}.%").last if sha1
49:           # Fallback for old posts that don't contain data-video-base62-sha1
50:           thumbnail = Upload.where("original_filename LIKE ?", "#{url}.%").last if thumbnail.nil? &&
51:             sha1.nil?
52:           sha1 = thumbnail.sha1 if thumbnail
53:         end
54:         map[url] = sha1 if sha1
55:       end
56: 
57:       if map.length > 0
58:         reverse_map = {}
59: 
60:         map.each do |key, value|
61:           reverse_map[value] ||= []
62:           reverse_map[value] << key
63:         end
64: 
65:         Upload
66:           .where(sha1: map.values)
67:           .pluck(:sha1, :url, :extension, :secure)
68:           .each do |row|
69:             sha1, url, extension, secure = row
70: 
71:             if short_urls = reverse_map[sha1]
72:               secure_uploads = SiteSetting.secure_uploads? && secure
73: 
74:               short_urls.each do |short_url|
75:                 result[short_url] = {
76:                   url:
77:                     (
78:                       if secure_uploads
79:                         Upload.secure_uploads_url_from_upload_url(url)
80:                       else
81:                         Discourse.store.cdn_url(url)
82:                       end
83:                     ),
84:                   short_path: Upload.short_path(sha1: sha1, extension: extension),
85:                   base62_sha1: Upload.base62_sha1(sha1),
86:                 }
87:               end
88:             end
89:           end
90:       end
91: 
92:       result
93:     end
94: 
95:     def get_topic_info(topic_id)
96:       return unless topic_id.is_a?(Integer)
97:       # TODO this only handles public topics, secured one do not get this
98:       topic = Topic.find_by(id: topic_id)
99:       if topic && Guardian.new.can_see?(topic)
100:         { title: Rack::Utils.escape_html(topic.title), href: topic.url }
101:       elsif topic
102:         { title: I18n.t("on_another_topic"), href: topic.slugless_url }
103:       end
104:     end
105: 
106:     def hashtag_lookup(slug, cooking_user_id, types_in_priority_order)
107:       # NOTE: This is _somewhat_ expected since we need to be able to cook posts
108:       # etc. without a user sometimes, but it is still an edge case.
109:       #
110:       # The Discourse.system_user is usually an admin with access to _all_
111:       # categories, however if the suppress_secured_categories_from_admin
112:       # site setting is activated then this user will not be able to access
113:       # secure categories, so hashtags that are secure will not render.
114:       cooking_user = User.find_by(id: cooking_user_id) || Discourse.system_user
115: 
116:       types_in_priority_order =
117:         types_in_priority_order.select do |type|
118:           HashtagAutocompleteService.data_source_types.include?(type)
119:         end

70:           !!(url =~ Regexp.new("^#{Discourse.base_path}/(assets|plugins|images)/")) ||

74: 
75:   def self.absolute(url, cdn = Discourse.asset_host)
76:     cdn = "https:#{cdn}" if cdn && cdn =~ %r{\A//}
77:     url =~ %r{\A/[^/]} ? (cdn || Discourse.base_url_no_prefix) + url : url
78:   end
79: 
80:   def self.absolute_without_cdn(url)
81:     self.absolute(url, nil)
82:   end
83: 
84:   def self.schemaless(url)
85:     url.sub(/\Ahttp:/i, "")
86:   end
87: 
88:   def self.secure_proxy_without_cdn(url)
89:     self.absolute(Upload.secure_uploads_url_from_upload_url(url), nil)
90:   end
91: 
92:   def self.normalized_encode(uri)
93:     url = uri.to_s
94: 
95:     if url.length > MAX_URL_LENGTH
96:       raise ArgumentError.new(
97:               "URL is too long (#{url.length} characters): #{url.truncate(MAX_URL_LENGTH)}",
98:             )
99:     end
100: 
101:     # Ideally we will jump straight to `Addressable::URI.normalized_encode`. However,
102:     # that implementation has some edge-case issues like https://github.com/sporkmonger/addressable/issues/472.
103:     # To temporarily work around those issues for the majority of cases, we try parsing with `::URI`.
104:     # If that fails (e.g. due to non-ascii characters) then we will fall back to addressable.
105:     # Hopefully we can simplify this back to `Addressable::URI.normalized_encode` in the future.
106: 
107:     # edge case where we expect mailto:test%40test.com to normalize to mailto:test@test.com
108:     return normalize_with_addressable(url) if url.match(/\Amailto:/)
109: 
110:     # If it doesn't pass the regexp, it's definitely not gonna parse with URI.parse. Skip
111:     # to addressable
112:     return normalize_with_addressable(url) if !url.match?(/\A#{URI.regexp}\z/)
113: 
114:     begin
115:       normalize_with_ruby_uri(url)
116:     rescue URI::Error
117:       normalize_with_addressable(url)
118:     end
119:   end
120: 
121:   def self.rails_route_from_url(url)
122:     path = URI.parse(encode(url)).path
123:     Rails.application.routes.recognize_path(path)
124:   rescue Addressable::URI::InvalidURIError, URI::InvalidComponentError
125:     nil
126:   end
127: 
128:   def self.cook_url(url, secure: false, local: nil)
129:     is_secure = SiteSetting.secure_uploads && secure
130:     local = is_local(url) if local.nil?
131:     return url if !local
132: 
133:     url = is_secure ? secure_proxy_without_cdn(url) : absolute_without_cdn(url)
134: 
135:     # we always want secure uploads to come from
136:     # Discourse.base_url_no_prefix/secure-uploads
137:     # to avoid asset_host mixups
138:     return schemaless(url) if is_secure
139: 
140:     # PERF: avoid parsing url except for extreme conditions
141:     # this is a hot path used on home page
142:     filename = url
143:     if url.include?("?")
144:       uri = URI.parse(url)
145:       filename = File.basename(uri.path)
146:     end
147: 
148:     # this technically requires a filename, but will work with a URL as long as it end with the
149:     # extension and has no query params
150:     is_attachment = !FileHelper.is_supported_media?(filename)
151: 
152:     no_cdn = SiteSetting.login_required || SiteSetting.prevent_anons_from_downloading_files
153:     unless is_attachment && no_cdn
154:       url = Discourse.store.cdn_url(url)
155:       url = local_cdn_url(url) if Discourse.store.external?
156:     end
157: 
158:     schemaless(url)
159:   rescue URI::Error
160:     url
161:   end
162: 
163:   def self.local_cdn_url(url)
164:     return url if Discourse.asset_host.blank?
165:     if url.start_with?("/#{Discourse.store.upload_path}/")
166:       "#{Discourse.asset_host}#{url}"
167:     else
168:       url.sub(Discourse.base_url_no_prefix, Discourse.asset_host)
169:     end
170:   end
171: 
172:   private
173: 
174:   def self.normalize_with_addressable(url)
175:     u = Addressable::URI.normalized_encode(url, Addressable::URI)
176: 
177:     u.host = ::Addressable::IDNA.to_ascii(u.host) if u.host && !u.host.ascii_only?
178: 
179:     u.to_s
180:   end
181: 
182:   def self.normalize_with_ruby_uri(url)
183:     u = URI.parse(url)
184: 
185:     u.scheme = u.scheme.downcase if u.scheme && u.scheme != u.scheme.downcase
186:

3: module GlobalPath
4:   def path(p)
5:     "#{GlobalSetting.relative_url_root}#{p}"
6:   end
7: 
8:   def cdn_path(p)
9:     GlobalSetting.cdn_url.blank? ? p : "#{GlobalSetting.cdn_url}#{path(p)}"
10:   end
11: 
12:   def upload_cdn_path(p)
13:     p = Discourse.store.cdn_url(p) if SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url.present?
14: 
15:     (p =~ /\Ahttp/ || p =~ %r{\A//}) ? p : cdn_path(p)

17: 
18:   def cdn_relative_path(path)
19:     if (cdn_url = GlobalSetting.cdn_url).present?
20:       URI.parse(cdn_url).path + path
21:     else
22:       path
23:     end
24:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: ##
4: # There are certain conditions with secure uploads when the security of
5: # uploads will need to change depending on the context they reside in.
6: #
7: # For example on these conditions:
8: #  * Topic category change
9: #  * Topic switching between PM and public topic
10: #  * Post moving between topics
11: #
12: # We need to go through all of the posts in that topic that
13: # own uploads via access_control_post_id, then for those uploads determine
14: # if they still need to be secure or not. For example an upload could be
15: # secure if it is in a PM, and then when the topic gets converted to a public
16: # topic the upload no longer needs to remain secure as it is no longer in
17: # a secure context.
18: class TopicUploadSecurityManager
19:   def initialize(topic)
20:     @topic = topic
21:   end
22: 
23:   def run
24:     rebaked_posts = []
25:     Rails.logger.debug("Updating upload security in topic #{@topic.id}")
26:     posts_owning_uploads.each do |post|
27:       Post.transaction do
28:         Rails.logger.debug("Updating upload security in topic #{@topic.id} - post #{post.id}")
29:         post.topic = @topic
30: 
31:         secure_status_did_change =
32:           post.owned_uploads_via_access_control.any? do |upload|
33:             # We already have the post preloaded so we may as well
34:             # attach it here to avoid another load in UploadSecurity
35:             # (which is called via update_secure_status)
36:             upload.access_control_post = post
37:             upload.update_secure_status(source: "topic upload security")
38:           end
39: 
40:         if secure_status_did_change
41:           post.rebake!
42:           rebaked_posts << post
43:         end
44:         Rails.logger.debug(
45:           "Security updated & rebake complete in topic #{@topic.id} - post #{post.id}",
46:         )
47:       end
48:     end
49: 
50:     return rebaked_posts if !SiteSetting.secure_uploads
51: 
52:     # We only want to do this if secure uploads is enabled. If
53:     # the setting is turned on after a site has been running
54:     # already, we want to make sure that any post moves after
55:     # this are handled and upload secure statuses and ACLs
56:     # are updated appropriately, as well as setting the access control
57:     # post for secure uploads missing it.
58:     #
59:     # Examples (all after secure uploads is enabled):
60:     #
61:     #  -> a public topic is moved to a private category after
62:     #  -> a PM is converted to a public topic
63:     #  -> a public topic is converted to a PM
64:     #  -> a topic is moved from a private to a public category
65:     posts_with_unowned_uploads.each do |post|
66:       Post.transaction do
67:         Rails.logger.debug(
68:           "Setting upload access control posts in topic #{@topic.id} - post #{post.id}",
69:         )
70:         post.topic = @topic
71: 
72:         secure_status_did_change =
73:           post.uploads.any? do |upload|
74:             first_post_upload_appeared_in =
75:               upload.upload_references.where(target_type: "Post").first.target
76:             if first_post_upload_appeared_in == post
77:               upload.update(access_control_post: post)
78:               upload.update_secure_status(source: "topic upload security")
79:             else
80:               false
81:             end
82:           end
83: 
84:         if secure_status_did_change
85:           post.rebake!
86:           rebaked_posts << post
87:         end
88:         Rails.logger.debug(
89:           "Completed changing access control posts #{secure_status_did_change ? "and rebaking" : ""} in topic #{@topic.id} - post #{post.id}",
90:         )
91:       end
92:     end
93: 
94:     Rails.logger.debug("Completed updating upload security in topic #{@topic.id}!")
95:     rebaked_posts
96:   end
97: 
98:   private
99: 
100:   def posts_owning_uploads
101:     Post.where(topic_id: @topic.id, id: Upload.select(:access_control_post_id))
102:   end
103: 
104:   def posts_with_unowned_uploads
105:     Post.where(
106:       topic_id: @topic.id,
107:       id:
108:         UploadReference.where(
109:           target_type: "Post",
110:           upload: Upload.where(access_control_post_id: nil),
111:         ).select(:target_id),
112:     ).includes(:uploads)
113:   end
114: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "aws-sdk-s3"
4: require "csv"
5: 
6: class S3Inventory
7:   attr_reader :type, :inventory_date, :s3_helper
8: 
9:   CSV_KEY_INDEX = 1
10:   CSV_ETAG_INDEX = 2
11:   INVENTORY_PREFIX = "inventory"
12:   INVENTORY_LAG = 2.days
13:   WAIT_AFTER_RESTORE_DAYS = 2
14: 
15:   def initialize(
16:     type,
17:     s3_inventory_bucket:,
18:     preloaded_inventory_file: nil,
19:     preloaded_inventory_date: nil,
20:     s3_options: {}
21:   )
22:     @s3_helper = S3Helper.new(s3_inventory_bucket, "", s3_options)
23: 
24:     if preloaded_inventory_file && preloaded_inventory_date
25:       # Data preloaded, so we don't need to fetch it again
26:       @preloaded_inventory_file = preloaded_inventory_file
27:       @inventory_date = preloaded_inventory_date
28:     end
29: 
30:     if type == :upload
31:       @type = "original"
32:       @model = Upload
33:       @scope = @model.by_users.without_s3_file_missing_confirmed_verification_status
34:     elsif type == :optimized
35:       @type = "optimized"
36:       @scope = @model = OptimizedImage
37:     else
38:       raise "Invalid type: #{type}"
39:     end
40:   end
41: 
42:   def backfill_etags_and_list_missing
43:     if !@preloaded_inventory_file && files.blank?
44:       error("Failed to list inventory from S3")
45:       return
46:     end
47: 
48:     DistributedMutex.synchronize("s3_inventory_list_missing_#{type}", validity: 30.minutes) do
49:       begin
50:         download_and_decompress_files if !@preloaded_inventory_file
51: 
52:         multisite_prefix = Discourse.store.upload_path
53: 
54:         ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
55:           begin
56:             connection.exec(
57:               "CREATE TEMP TABLE #{tmp_table_name}(url text UNIQUE, etag text, PRIMARY KEY(etag, url))",
58:             )
59: 
60:             connection.copy_data("COPY #{tmp_table_name} FROM STDIN CSV") do
61:               for_each_inventory_row do |row|
62:                 key = row[CSV_KEY_INDEX]
63: 
64:                 next if Rails.configuration.multisite && key.exclude?(multisite_prefix)
65:                 next if key.exclude?("#{type}/")
66: 
67:                 url = File.join(Discourse.store.absolute_base_url, key)
68:                 connection.put_copy_data("#{CGI.unescape(url)},#{row[CSV_ETAG_INDEX]}\n")
69:               end
70:             end
71: 
72:             table_name = @model.table_name
73: 
74:             # backfilling etags
75:             connection.async_exec(
76:               "UPDATE #{table_name}
77:               SET etag = #{tmp_table_name}.etag
78:               FROM #{tmp_table_name}
79:               WHERE #{table_name}.etag IS NULL AND
80:                 #{table_name}.url = #{tmp_table_name}.url",
81:             )
82: 
83:             uploads = @scope.where("updated_at < ?", inventory_date)
84: 
85:             missing_uploads =
86:               uploads.joins(
87:                 "LEFT JOIN #{tmp_table_name} ON #{tmp_table_name}.etag = #{table_name}.etag",
88:               ).where("#{tmp_table_name}.etag IS NULL")
89: 
90:             exists_with_different_etag =
91:               missing_uploads
92:                 .joins(
93:                   "LEFT JOIN #{tmp_table_name} inventory2 ON inventory2.url = #{table_name}.url",
94:                 )
95:                 .where.not(inventory2: { etag: nil })
96:                 .pluck(:id)
97: 
98:             # marking as verified/not verified
99:             if @model == Upload
100:               sql_params = {
101:                 inventory_date: inventory_date,
102:                 invalid_etag: Upload.verification_statuses[:invalid_etag],
103:                 s3_file_missing_confirmed: Upload.verification_statuses[:s3_file_missing_confirmed],
104:                 verified: Upload.verification_statuses[:verified],
105:                 seeded_id_threshold: @model::SEEDED_ID_THRESHOLD,
106:               }
107: 
108:               DB.exec(<<~SQL, sql_params)
109:                 UPDATE #{table_name}
110:                 SET verification_status = :verified
111:                 WHERE etag IS NOT NULL
112:                   AND verification_status <> :verified
113:                   AND verification_status <> :s3_file_missing_confirmed
114:                   AND updated_at < :inventory_date
115:                   AND id > :seeded_id_threshold
116:                   AND EXISTS
117:                     (
118:                         SELECT 1
119:                         FROM #{tmp_table_name}
120:                         WHERE #{tmp_table_name}.etag = #{table_name}.etag
121:                     )
122:               SQL
123: 
124:               DB.exec(<<~SQL, sql_params)
125:                 UPDATE #{table_name}
126:                 SET verification_status = :invalid_etag
127:                 WHERE verification_status <> :invalid_etag
128:                   AND verification_status <> :s3_file_missing_confirmed
129:                   AND updated_at < :inventory_date
130:                   AND id > :seeded_id_threshold

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class UploadRecovery
4:   def initialize(dry_run: false, stop_on_error: false)
5:     @dry_run = dry_run
6:     @stop_on_error = stop_on_error
7:   end
8: 
9:   def recover(posts = Post)
10:     posts.have_uploads.find_each { |post| recover_post post }
11:   end
12: 
13:   def recover_post(post)
14:     begin
15:       analyzer = PostAnalyzer.new(post.raw, post.topic_id)
16: 
17:       analyzer
18:         .cooked_stripped
19:         .css("img", "a")
20:         .each do |media|
21:           if media.name == "img" && orig_src = media["data-orig-src"]
22:             if dom_class = media["class"]
23:               next if (Post.allowed_image_classes & dom_class.split).count > 0
24:             end
25: 
26:             if @dry_run
27:               puts "#{post.full_url} #{orig_src}"
28:             else
29:               recover_post_upload(post, Upload.sha1_from_short_url(orig_src))
30:             end
31:           elsif url = (media["href"] || media["src"])
32:             data = Upload.extract_url(url)
33:             next unless data
34: 
35:             upload = Upload.get_from_url(url)
36: 
37:             if !upload || upload.verification_status == Upload.verification_statuses[:invalid_etag]
38:               if @dry_run
39:                 puts "#{post.full_url} #{url}"
40:               else
41:                 sha1 = data[2]
42:                 recover_post_upload(post, sha1)
43:               end
44:             end
45:           end
46:         end
47:     rescue => e
48:       raise e if @stop_on_error
49:       puts "#{post.full_url} #{e.class}: #{e.message}"
50:     end
51:   end
52: 
53:   private
54: 
55:   def recover_post_upload(post, sha1)
56:     return unless valid_sha1?(sha1)
57: 
58:     attributes = { post: post, sha1: sha1 }
59: 
60:     if Discourse.store.external?
61:       recover_post_upload_from_s3(**attributes)
62:     else
63:       recover_post_upload_from_local(**attributes)
64:     end
65:   end
66: 
67:   def ensure_upload!(post:, sha1:, upload:)
68:     return if !upload.persisted?
69: 
70:     if upload.sha1 != sha1
71:       STDERR.puts "Warning #{post.url} had an incorrect #{sha1} should be #{upload.sha1} storing in custom field 'rake uploads:fix_relative_upload_links' can fix this"
72: 
73:       sha_map = post.custom_fields["UPLOAD_SHA1_MAP"] || "{}"
74:       sha_map = JSON.parse(sha_map)
75:       sha_map[sha1] = upload.sha1
76: 
77:       post.custom_fields["UPLOAD_SHA1_MAP"] = sha_map.to_json
78:       post.save_custom_fields
79:     end
80: 
81:     post.rebake!
82:   end
83: 
84:   def recover_post_upload_from_local(post:, sha1:)
85:     recover_from_local(sha1: sha1, user_id: post.user_id) do |upload|
86:       ensure_upload!(post: post, sha1: sha1, upload: upload)
87:     end
88:   end
89: 
90:   def recover_post_upload_from_s3(post:, sha1:)
91:     recover_from_s3(sha1: sha1, user_id: post.user_id) do |upload|
92:       ensure_upload!(post: post, sha1: sha1, upload: upload)
93:     end
94:   end
95: 
96:   def recover_from_local(sha1:, user_id:)
97:     @paths ||=
98:       begin
99:         Dir.glob(File.join(Discourse.store.tombstone_dir, "original", "**", "*.*")).concat(
100:           Dir.glob(File.join(Discourse.store.upload_path, "original", "**", "*.*")),
101:         )
102:       end
103: 
104:     @paths.each do |path|
105:       if path =~ /#{sha1}/
106:         begin
107:           tmp = Tempfile.new
108:           tmp.write(File.read(path))
109:           tmp.rewind
110: 
111:           upload = create_upload(tmp, File.basename(path), user_id)
112:           yield upload if block_given?
113:         ensure
114:           tmp&.close
115:         end
116:       end
117:     end
118:   end
119: 
120:   def recover_from_s3(sha1:, user_id:)
121:     @object_keys ||=
122:       begin
123:         s3_helper = Discourse.store.s3_helper
124: 
125:         if Rails.configuration.multisite
126:           current_db = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
127:           s3_helper
128:             .list("uploads/#{current_db}/original")
129:             .map(&:key)
130:             .concat(
131:               s3_helper.list(
132:                 "uploads/#{FileStore::S3Store::TOMBSTONE_PREFIX}#{current_db}/original",
133:               ).map(&:key),
134:             )
135:         else
136:           s3_helper
137:             .list("original")
138:             .map(&:key)
139:             .concat(s3_helper.list("#{FileStore::S3Store::TOMBSTONE_PREFIX}original").map(&:key))
140:         end
141:       end
142: 
143:     upload_exists = Upload.exists?(sha1: sha1)
144: 
145:     @object_keys.each do |key|
146:       if key =~ /#{sha1}/
147:         tombstone_prefix = FileStore::S3Store::TOMBSTONE_PREFIX
148: 
149:         if key.include?(tombstone_prefix)
150:           old_key = key
151:           key = key.sub(tombstone_prefix, "")
152:           Discourse.store.copy_file(source: old_key, destination: key, secure: false)
153:         end
154: 
155:         next if upload_exists
156: 
157:         url = "https:#{SiteSetting.Upload.absolute_base_url}/#{key}"
158: 
159:         begin
160:           tmp =
161:             FileHelper.download(
162:               url,
163:               max_file_size: SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes,
164:               tmp_file_name: "recover_from_s3",
165:             )
166: 
167:           if tmp
168:             upload = create_upload(tmp, File.basename(key), user_id)
169:             yield upload if block_given?
170:           end
171:         ensure
172:           tmp&.close
173:         end
174:       end
175:     end
176:   end
177: 
178:   def create_upload(file, filename, user_id)
179:     UploadCreator.new(file, filename).create_for(user_id)
180:   end
181: 
182:   def valid_sha1?(sha1)
183:     sha1.present? && sha1.length == Upload::SHA1_LENGTH
184:   end
185: end

(End of file - total 185 lines)
</content>

6:   end

10:     return if upload_id.blank?
11: 
12:     cdn_url(upload_id)
13:   end
14: 
15:   def value=(new_value)

17:       if new_value == default
18:         new_value = default_upload_id
19:       else
20:         upload = ::Upload.find_by(url: new_value)
21:         new_value = upload.id if upload.present?

80:   extend GlobalPath
81:   extend SiteSettingExtension
82: 
83:   has_many :upload_references, as: :target, dependent: :destroy
84: 
85:   validates :name, presence: true
86:   validates :data_type, presence: true
87: 
88:   after_save do
89:     if saved_change_to_value?
90:       if self.data_type == SiteSettings::TypeSupervisor.types[:upload]
91:         UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: [self.value], target: self)
92:       elsif self.data_type == SiteSettings::TypeSupervisor.types[:uploaded_image_list]
93:         upload_ids = self.value.split("|").compact.uniq
94:         UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: upload_ids, target: self)
95:       elsif self.data_type == SiteSettings::TypeSupervisor.types[:objects] && self.value.present?
96:         upload_values =
97:           SchemaSettingsObjectValidator.property_values_of_type(
98:             schema: SiteSetting.type_supervisor.type_hash(self.name.to_sym)[:schema],
99:             objects: JSON.parse(self.value),
100:             type: "upload",
101:           )
102: 
103:         # Convert URLs to upload IDs (values can be either integer IDs or URL strings)
104:         upload_ids =
105:           upload_values.filter_map do |value|
106:             if value.is_a?(Integer)
107:               value
108:             elsif value.is_a?(String) && value.present?
109:               ::Upload.get_from_url(value)&.id
110:             end
111:           end
112: 
113:         # Always call ensure_exist! to clean up old references when uploads are removed
114:         UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: upload_ids, target: self)
115:       end
116:     end
117:   end
118: 
119:   load_settings(File.join(Rails.root, "config", "site_settings.yml"))
120: 
121:   if Rails.env.test?
122:     SAMPLE_TEST_PLUGIN =
123:       Plugin::Instance.new(
124:         Plugin::Metadata.new.tap { |metadata| metadata.name = "discourse-sample-plugin" },
125:       )
126: 
127:     Discourse.plugins_by_name[SAMPLE_TEST_PLUGIN.name] = SAMPLE_TEST_PLUGIN
128: 
129:     load_settings(
130:       File.join(Rails.root, "spec", "support", "sample_plugin_site_settings.yml"),
131:       plugin: SAMPLE_TEST_PLUGIN.name,
132:     )
133:   end
134: 
135:   if GlobalSetting.load_plugins?
136:     Dir[File.join(Rails.root, "plugins", "*", "config", "settings.yml")].each do |file|
137:       load_settings(file, plugin: file.split("/")[-3])
138:     end
139: 
140:     # Sometimes plugins need to define their own fake site settings for testing
141:     if Rails.env.test?
142:       Dir[
143:         File.join(Rails.root, "plugins", "*", "spec", "support", "dummy_plugin_site_settings.yml")
144:       ].each { |file| load_settings(file, plugin: file.split("/")[-4]) }
145:     end
146:   end
147: 
148:   setup_deprecated_methods
149:   client_settings << :available_locales
150: 
151:   def self.available_locales
152:     LocaleSiteSetting.values.to_json
153:   end
154: 
155:   def self.content_localization_locales
156:     locales = SiteSetting.content_localization_supported_locales.split("|")
157:     default_locale = SiteSetting.default_locale
158:     locales << default_locale if default_locale.present? && !locales.include?(default_locale)
159:     locales
160:   end
161: 
162:   client_settings << :available_content_localization_locales
163: 
164:   def self.available_content_localization_locales
165:     return [] if !SiteSetting.content_localization_enabled?
166: 
167:     locales = content_localization_locales

1381:       when "objects"

1383:         if nested_objects.is_a?(Array)
1384:           nested_objects.each do |nested_obj|
1385:             hydrate_uploads_in_object(nested_obj, prop_value[:schema][:properties], uploads_by_id)
1386:           end

47:     end
48:

52:     end
53: 
54:     ww, hh = ImageSizer.resize(w, h)

60:     upload.attributes = {
61:       sha1: sha1,

67:     }

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class SiteSetting::SplashScreenImageChanged
4:   include Service::Base
5: 
6:   params { attribute :upload_id, :integer }
7: 
8:   model :upload
9:   model :svg
10:   model :cleaned_svg
11:   step :save_cleaned_svg_upload
12:   step :clear_cache
13: 
14:   private
15: 
16:   def fetch_upload(params:)
17:     Upload.find_by(id: params.upload_id)
18:   end
19: 
20:   def fetch_svg(upload:)
21:     content =
22:       begin
23:         upload.content
24:       rescue StandardError
25:         nil
26:       end
27: 
28:     return nil if content.blank?
29: 
30:     Nokogiri.XML(content).at_css("svg")
31:   end
32: 
33:   def fetch_cleaned_svg(svg:)
34:     # Disables SMIL animations which can cause performance issues,
35:     # CSS animations are preferred.
36:     svg.xpath(
37:       ".//*[local-name()='animate' or local-name()='animateTransform' or local-name()='animateMotion' or local-name()='set']",
38:     ).each(&:remove)
39: 
40:     # Remove explicit dimensions so the SVG scales via viewBox
41:     if svg["viewBox"].present?
42:       svg.remove_attribute("width")
43:       svg.remove_attribute("height")
44:     end
45: 
46:     svg.to_xml
47:   end
48: 
49:   def save_cleaned_svg_upload(cleaned_svg:, upload:)
50:     return if cleaned_svg == upload.content
51: 
52:     Tempfile.open(%w[splash_screen .svg]) do |tmp|
53:       tmp.write(cleaned_svg)
54:       tmp.rewind
55: 
56:       new_sha1 = Upload.generate_digest(tmp.path)
57:       existing = Upload.find_by(sha1: new_sha1)
58: 
59:       if existing && existing.id != upload.id
60:         SiteSetting.splash_screen_image = existing.id
61:       else
62:         old_path = Discourse.store.get_path_for_upload(upload)
63:         old_url = upload.url
64:         upload.tap do |u|
65:           u.sha1 = new_sha1
66:           u.filesize = tmp.size
67:           u.url = Discourse.store.store_upload(tmp, u)
68:           u.save!(validate: false)
69:         end
70: 
71:         Discourse.store.remove_file(old_url, old_path) if upload.url != old_url
72:       end
73:     end
74:   end
75: 
76:   def clear_cache(upload:)
77:     Discourse.cache.delete("splash_screen_svg_#{upload.id}_#{upload.sha1}")
78:   end
79: end

146:     def dump_public_schema
147:       log "Dumping the public schema of the database..."
148: 
149:       logs = Queue.new
150:       pg_dump_running = true
151: 
152:       Thread.new do
153:         RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.establish_connection(db: @current_db)
154:         while pg_dump_running
155:           message = logs.pop.strip
156:           log(message) if message.present?
157:         end
158:       end
159: 
160:       IO.popen("#{pg_dump_command} 2>&1") do |pipe|
161:         begin
162:           while line = pipe.readline
163:             logs << line
164:           end
165:         rescue EOFError
166:           # finished reading...
167:         ensure
168:           pg_dump_running = false
169:           logs << ""
170:         end
171:       end
172: 
173:       raise "pg_dump failed" unless $?.success?
174:     end
175: 
176:     def pg_dump_command
177:       db_conf = BackupRestore.database_configuration
178: 
179:       password_argument = "PGPASSWORD='#{db_conf.password}'" if db_conf.password.present?
180:       host_argument = "--host=#{db_conf.host}" if db_conf.host.present?
181:       port_argument = "--port=#{db_conf.port}" if db_conf.port.present?
182:       username_argument = "--username=#{db_conf.username}" if db_conf.username.present?
183: 
184:       [
185:         password_argument, # pass the password to pg_dump (if any)
186:         "pg_dump", # the pg_dump command
187:         "--schema=public", # only public schema
188:         "-T public.pg_*", # exclude tables and views whose name starts with "pg_"
189:         "--file='#{@dump_filename}'", # output to the dump.sql file
190:         "--no-owner", # do not output commands to set ownership of objects
191:         "--no-privileges", # prevent dumping of access privileges
192:         "--verbose", # specifies verbose mode
193:         "--compress=4", # Compression level of 4
194:         host_argument, # the hostname to connect to (if any)
195:         port_argument, # the port to connect to (if any)
196:         username_argument, # the username to connect as (if any)
197:         db_conf.database, # the name of the database to dump
198:       ].join(" ")
199:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module BackupRestore
4:   UploadsRestoreError = Class.new(RuntimeError)
5: 
6:   class UploadsRestorer
7:     delegate :log, to: :@logger, private: true
8: 
9:     S3_ENDPOINT_REGEX = /\.s3(?:\.dualstack\.[a-z0-9\-]+?|[.\-][a-z0-9\-]+?)?\.amazonaws\.com/
10: 
11:     def self.s3_regex_string(s3_base_url)
12:       clean_url = s3_base_url.sub(S3_ENDPOINT_REGEX, ".s3.amazonaws.com")
13: 
14:       regex_string =
15:         clean_url
16:           .split(".s3.amazonaws.com")
17:           .map { |s| Regexp.escape(s) }
18:           .insert(1, S3_ENDPOINT_REGEX.source)
19:           .join("")
20: 
21:       [regex_string, clean_url]
22:     end
23: 
24:     def initialize(logger)
25:       @logger = logger
26:     end
27: 
28:     def restore(tmp_directory)
29:       upload_directories =
30:         Dir
31:           .glob(File.join(tmp_directory, "uploads", "*"))
32:           .reject { |path| File.basename(path).start_with?("PaxHeaders") }
33: 
34:       if upload_directories.count > 1
35:         raise UploadsRestoreError.new(
36:                 "Could not find uploads, because the uploads " \
37:                   "directory contains multiple folders.",
38:               )
39:       end
40: 
41:       @tmp_uploads_path = upload_directories.first
42:       return if @tmp_uploads_path.blank?
43: 
44:       @previous_db_name = BackupMetadata.value_for("db_name") || File.basename(@tmp_uploads_path)
45:       @current_db_name = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
46:       backup_contains_optimized_images = File.exist?(File.join(@tmp_uploads_path, "optimized"))
47: 
48:       remap_uploads
49:       restore_uploads
50: 
51:       generate_optimized_images unless backup_contains_optimized_images
52:       rebake_posts_with_uploads
53:     end
54: 
55:     protected
56: 
57:     def restore_uploads
58:       store = Discourse.store
59: 
60:       if !store.respond_to?(:copy_from)
61:         # a FileStore implementation from a plugin might not support this method, so raise a helpful error
62:         store_name = Discourse.store.class.name
63:         raise UploadsRestoreError.new(
64:                 "The current file store (#{store_name}) does not support restoring uploads.",
65:               )
66:       end
67: 
68:       log "Restoring uploads, this may take a while..."
69:       store.copy_from(@tmp_uploads_path)
70:     end
71: 
72:     # Remaps upload URLs depending on old and new configuration.
73:     # URLs of uploads differ a little bit between local uploads and uploads stored on S3.
74:     # Multisites are another reason why URLs can be different.
75:     #
76:     # Examples:
77:     #   * regular site, local storage
78:     #     /uploads/default/original/1X/63b76551662ccea1a594e161c37dd35188d77657.jpeg
79:     #
80:     #   * regular site, S3
81:     #     //bucket-name.s3.dualstack.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/original/1X/63b76551662ccea1a594e161c37dd35188d77657.jpeg
82:     #
83:     #   * multisite, local storage
84:     #     /uploads/<site-name>/original/1X/63b76551662ccea1a594e161c37dd35188d77657.jpeg
85:     #
86:     #   * multisite, S3
87:     #     //bucket-name.s3.dualstack.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/uploads/<site-name>/original/1X/63b76551662ccea1a594e161c37dd35188d77657.jpeg
88:     def remap_uploads
89:       log "Remapping uploads..."
90: 
91:       was_multisite = BackupMetadata.value_for("multisite") == "t"
92:       upload_path = "/#{Discourse.store.upload_path}/"
93:       uploads_folder = was_multisite ? "/" : upload_path
94: 
95:       if (old_base_url = BackupMetadata.value_for("base_url")) && old_base_url != Discourse.base_url
96:         remap(old_base_url, Discourse.base_url)
97:       end
98: 
99:       current_s3_base_url =
100:         SiteSetting::Upload.enable_s3_uploads ? SiteSetting::Upload.s3_base_url : nil
101:       if (old_s3_base_url = BackupMetadata.value_for("s3_base_url")) &&
102:            old_s3_base_url != current_s3_base_url
103:         remap_s3("#{old_s3_base_url}/", uploads_folder)
104:       end
105: 
106:       current_s3_cdn_url =
107:         SiteSetting::Upload.enable_s3_uploads ? SiteSetting::Upload.s3_cdn_url : nil
108:       if (old_s3_cdn_url = BackupMetadata.value_for("s3_cdn_url")) &&
109:            old_s3_cdn_url != current_s3_cdn_url
110:         base_url = current_s3_cdn_url || Discourse.base_url
111:         remap("#{old_s3_cdn_url}/", UrlHelper.schemaless("#{base_url}#{uploads_folder}"))
112: 
113:         old_host = URI.parse(old_s3_cdn_url).host
114:         new_host = URI.parse(base_url).host
115:         remap(old_host, new_host) if old_host != new_host
116:       end
117: 
118:       if (old_cdn_url = BackupMetadata.value_for("cdn_url")) && old_cdn_url != Discourse.asset_host
119:         base_url = Discourse.asset_host || Discourse.base_url
120:         remap("#{old_cdn_url}/", UrlHelper.schemaless("#{base_url}/"))

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Chat
4:   class MessageProcessor
5:     include ::CookedProcessorMixin
6:     IMG_FILETYPES = %w[jpg jpeg gif png heic heif webp]
7: 
8:     def initialize(chat_message, opts = {})
9:       @model = chat_message
10:       @previous_cooked = (chat_message.cooked || "").dup
11:       @should_secure_uploads = false
12:       @size_cache = {}
13:       @opts = opts
14: 
15:       cook_opts = { user_id: chat_message.last_editor_id }
16:       cook_opts[:invalidate_oneboxes] = true if opts[:invalidate_oneboxes] == true
17:       cooked = Chat::Message.cook(chat_message.message, **cook_opts)
18:       @doc = Loofah.html5_fragment(cooked)
19:     end
20: 
21:     def run!
22:       post_process_oneboxes
23:       process_thumbnails
24:       post_process_videos
25:       add_lightbox_to_images
26:       DiscourseEvent.trigger(:chat_message_processed, @doc, @model)
27:     end
28: 
29:     def process_thumbnails
30:       return if !SiteSetting.create_thumbnails
31: 
32:       @model.uploads.each do |upload|
33:         next if upload.blank? || IMG_FILETYPES.exclude?(upload.extension&.downcase)
34: 
35:         if upload.width <= SiteSetting.max_image_width &&
36:              upload.height <= SiteSetting.max_image_height
37:           return false
38:         end
39: 
40:         crop =
41:           SiteSetting.min_ratio_to_crop > 0 &&
42:             upload.width.to_f / upload.height.to_f < SiteSetting.min_ratio_to_crop
43: 
44:         width = upload.thumbnail_width
45:         height = upload.thumbnail_height
46: 
47:         # create the main thumbnail
48:         upload.create_thumbnail!(width, height, crop: crop)
49: 
50:         # create additional responsive thumbnails
51:         each_responsive_ratio do |ratio|
52:           resized_w = (width * ratio).to_i
53:           resized_h = (height * ratio).to_i
54: 
55:           if upload.width && resized_w <= upload.width
56:             upload.create_thumbnail!(resized_w, resized_h, crop: crop)
57:           end
58:         end
59:       end
60:     end
61: 
62:     def add_lightbox_to_images
63:       @doc
64:         .css("img")
65:         .each do |img|
66:           if img["class"]&.include?("emoji") || img["class"]&.include?("avatar") ||
67:                img["data-base62-sha1"].blank?
68:             next
69:           end
70: 
71:           sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_base62_encoded(img["data-base62-sha1"])
72:           if upload = Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
73:             img["data-large-src"] = UrlHelper.cook_url(upload.url, secure: upload.secure?)
74:             img["data-download-href"] = upload.short_path
75:             img["data-target-width"] = upload.width
76:             img["data-target-height"] = upload.height
77:             img["class"] = "#{img["class"]} lightbox".strip
78:           end
79:         end
80:     end
81: 
82:     def large_images
83:       []
84:     end
85: 
86:     def broken_images
87:       []
88:     end
89: 
90:     def downloaded_images
91:       {}
92:     end
93: 
94:     def post_process_videos
95:       changes_made = false
96: 
97:       begin
98:         @doc
99:           .css(".video-placeholder-container")
100:           .each do |container|
101:             src = container["data-video-src"]
102:             next if src.blank?
103: 
104:             # Look for optimized video
105:             upload = Upload.get_from_url(src)
106:             if upload && optimized_video = OptimizedVideo.find_by(upload_id: upload.id)
107:               optimized_url = Discourse.store.cdn_url(optimized_video.optimized_upload.url)
108:               # Only update if the URL is different
109:               if container["data-video-src"] != optimized_url
110:                 container["data-original-video-src"] = container["data-video-src"] unless container[
111:                   "data-original-video-src"
112:                 ]
113:                 container["data-video-src"] = optimized_url
114:                 changes_made = true
115:               end
116:               # Ensure we maintain reference to original upload and optimized upload
117:               UploadReference.ensure_exist!(
118:                 upload_ids: [upload.id, optimized_video.optimized_upload.id],
119:                 target: @model,
120:               )
121:             end
122:           end
123: 
124:         # Handle video tags with source elements (for direct video uploads in cooked HTML)
125:         @doc
126:           .css("video.chat-video-upload source, video source")
127:           .each do |source|
128:             src = source["src"]
129:             next if src.blank?
130: 
131:             # Look for optimized video
132:             upload = Upload.get_from_url(src)
133:             if upload && optimized_video = OptimizedVideo.find_by(upload_id: upload.id)
134:               optimized_url = Discourse.store.cdn_url(optimized_video.optimized_upload.url)
135:               # Only update if the URL is different
136:               if source["src"] != optimized_url
137:                 source["data-original-src"] = source["src"] unless source["data-original-src"]
138:                 source["src"] = optimized_url
139:                 changes_made = true
140:               end
141:               # Ensure we maintain reference to original upload and optimized upload
142:               UploadReference.ensure_exist!(
143:                 upload_ids: [upload.id, optimized_video.optimized_upload.id],
144:                 target: @model,
145:               )
146:             end
147:           end
148: 
149:         # Also check uploads directly associated with the message
150:         @model.uploads.each do |upload|
151:           next unless FileHelper.is_supported_video?(upload.original_filename)
152: 
153:           if optimized_video = OptimizedVideo.find_by(upload_id: upload.id)
154:             # Ensure we maintain reference to optimized upload
155:             UploadReference.ensure_exist!(
156:               upload_ids: [optimized_video.optimized_upload.id],
157:               target: @model,
158:             )
159:           end
160:         end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module DiscourseAi
4:   module Completions
5:     class UploadEncoder
6:       def self.encode(
7:         upload_ids:,
8:         max_pixels:,
9:         allowed_kinds: [:image],
10:         allowed_attachment_types: nil
11:       )
12:         uploads = []
13:         allowed_attachment_types = normalize_attachment_types(allowed_attachment_types)
14:         upload_ids.each do |upload_id|
15:           upload = Upload.find(upload_id)
16:           next if upload.blank?
17: 
18:           extension = upload.extension&.downcase
19:           kind = image_extension?(extension) ? :image : :document
20: 
21:           next if allowed_kinds.exclude?(kind)
22: 
23:           if kind == :document
24:             mime_type =
25:               MiniMime.lookup_by_filename(upload.original_filename)&.content_type ||
26:                 "application/octet-stream"
27: 
28:             attachment_type = attachment_type_for(upload.extension, mime_type)
29:             next if disallowed_attachment?(allowed_attachment_types, attachment_type)
30: 
31:             payload = encode_document(upload, mime_type)
32:             uploads << payload if payload
33:             next
34:           end
35: 
36:           next if upload.width.to_i == 0 || upload.height.to_i == 0
37: 
38:           desired_extension = upload.extension
39:           desired_extension = "png" if upload.extension == "gif"
40:           desired_extension = "png" if upload.extension == "webp"
41:           desired_extension = "jpeg" if upload.extension == "jpg"
42: 
43:           # this keeps it very simple format wise given everyone supports png and jpg
44:           next if !%w[jpeg png].include?(desired_extension)
45: 
46:           payload = encode_image(upload, desired_extension, max_pixels)
47:           uploads << payload if payload
48:         end
49:         uploads
50:       end
51: 
52:       def self.attachment_type_for(extension, mime_type)
53:         ext = extension.to_s.delete_prefix(".").downcase
54:         mime = mime_type.to_s
55: 
56:         return "pdf" if ext == "pdf" || mime.include?("pdf")
57:         return "docx" if ext == "docx"
58:         return "doc" if ext == "doc"
59:         return "txt" if ext == "txt" || mime.include?("text/plain")
60:         return "rtf" if ext == "rtf"
61:         return "html" if %w[html htm].include?(ext) || mime.include?("html")
62:         return "markdown" if %w[md markdown].include?(ext) || mime.include?("markdown")
63: 
64:         "file"
65:       end
66: 
67:       class << self
68:         private
69: 
70:         def normalize_attachment_types(types)
71:           return nil if types.nil?
72: 
73:           Array(types).map(&:downcase)
74:         end
75: 
76:         def disallowed_attachment?(allowed_types, attachment_type)
77:           !allowed_types.nil? && !allowed_types.include?(attachment_type)
78:         end
79: 
80:         def image_extension?(ext)
81:           %w[jpg jpeg png gif webp].include?(ext)
82:         end
83: 
84:         def encode_document(upload, mime_type)
85:           path = fetch_path(upload)
86:           return if path.blank?
87: 
88:           encoded = Base64.strict_encode64(File.binread(path))
89: 
90:           {
91:             base64: encoded,
92:             mime_type: mime_type,
93:             kind: :document,
94:             filename: upload.original_filename,
95:           }
96:         end
97: 
98:         def encode_image(upload, desired_extension, max_pixels)
99:           original_pixels = upload.width * upload.height
100: 
101:           image = upload
102: 
103:           if original_pixels > max_pixels
104:             ratio = max_pixels.to_f / original_pixels
105: 
106:             new_width = (ratio * upload.width).to_i
107:             new_height = (ratio * upload.height).to_i
108: 
109:             image = upload.get_optimized_image(new_width, new_height, format: desired_extension)
110:           elsif upload.extension != desired_extension
111:             image =
112:               upload.get_optimized_image(upload.width, upload.height, format: desired_extension)
113:           end
114: 
115:           return if !image
116: 
117:           mime_type = MiniMime.lookup_by_filename("test.#{desired_extension}").content_type
118: 
119:           path = fetch_path(image)
120:           return if path.blank?
121: 
122:           encoded = Base64.strict_encode64(File.binread(path))
123: 
124:           {
125:             base64: encoded,
126:             mime_type: mime_type,
127:             kind: :image,
128:             filename: upload.original_filename,
129:           }
130:         end
131: 
132:         def fetch_path(upload)
133:           path = Discourse.store.path_for(upload)
134:           path = Discourse.store.download(upload) if path.blank?
135: 
136:           return if path.blank?
137:           return unless File.exist?(path)
138: 
139:           path
140:         end
141:       end
142:     end
143:   end
144: end

3: module DiscourseAi
4:   module Agents
5:     class ToolRunner
6:       module Upload
7:         def attach_upload(mini_racer_context)
8:           mini_racer_context.attach(
9:             "_upload_get_base64",
10:             ->(upload_id_or_url, max_pixels) do
11:               in_attached_function do
12:                 return nil if upload_id_or_url.blank?
13: 
14:                 upload = nil
15: 
16:                 # Handle both upload ID and short URL
17:                 if upload_id_or_url.to_s.start_with?("upload://")
18:                   # Handle short URL format
19:                   sha1 = ::Upload.sha1_from_short_url(upload_id_or_url)
20:                   return nil if sha1.blank?
21:                   upload = ::Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
22:                 else
23:                   # Handle numeric ID
24:                   upload_id = upload_id_or_url.to_i
25:                   return nil if upload_id <= 0
26:                   upload = ::Upload.find_by(id: upload_id)
27:                 end
28: 
29:                 return nil if upload.nil?
30: 
31:                 max_pixels = max_pixels&.to_i
32:                 max_pixels = nil if max_pixels && max_pixels <= 0
33: 
34:                 encoded_uploads =
35:                   DiscourseAi::Completions::UploadEncoder.encode(
36:                     upload_ids: [upload.id],
37:                     max_pixels: max_pixels || 10_000_000, # Default to 10M pixels if not specified
38:                   )
39: 
40:                 encoded_uploads.first&.dig(:base64)
41:               end
42:             end,
43:           )
44:           mini_racer_context.attach(
45:             "_upload_get_url",
46:             ->(short_url) do
47:               in_attached_function do
48:                 return nil if short_url.blank?
49: 
50:                 sha1 = ::Upload.sha1_from_short_url(short_url)
51:                 return nil if sha1.blank?
52: 
53:                 upload = ::Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
54:                 return nil if upload.nil?
55:                 # TODO we may need to introduce an API to unsecure, secure uploads
56:                 return nil if upload.secure?
57: 
58:                 GlobalPath.full_cdn_url(upload.url)
59:               end
60:             end,
61:           )
62:           mini_racer_context.attach(
63:             "_upload_create",
64:             ->(filename, base_64_content) do
65:               begin
66:                 in_attached_function do
67:                   # protect against misuse
68:                   filename = File.basename(filename)
69: 
70:                   Tempfile.create(filename) do |file|
71:                     file.binmode
72:                     file.write(Base64.decode64(base_64_content))
73:                     file.rewind
74: 
75:                     upload =
76:                       UploadCreator.new(
77:                         file,
78:                         filename,
79:                         for_private_message: @context.private_message,
80:                       ).create_for(@bot_user.id)
81: 
82:                     if upload&.persisted?
83:                       { "id" => upload.id, "short_url" => upload.short_url, "url" => upload.url }
84:                     else
85:                       error_msg =
86:                         upload&.errors&.full_messages&.join(", ") || "Upload creation failed"
87:                       { "error" => error_msg }
88:                     end
89:                   end
90:                 end
91:               rescue => e
92:                 { "error" => e.message }
93:               end
94:             end,
95:           )
96:         end
97:       end
98:     end
99:   end
100: end
