120:     class ImageTooLargeError < StandardError
121:     end
122: 
123:     class ImageBrokenError < StandardError
124:     end
125: 
126:     class UploadCreateError < StandardError
127:     end
128: 
129:     def attempt_download(src, user_id)
130:       # secure-uploads endpoint prevents anonymous downloads, so we
131:       # need the presigned S3 URL here
132:       if Upload.secure_uploads_url?(src)
133:         src = Upload.signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url(src, include_content_disposition: false)
134:       end
135: 
136:       hotlinked = download(src)
137:       raise ImageBrokenError if !hotlinked
138:       if File.size(hotlinked.path) > SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes
139:         raise ImageTooLargeError
140:       end
141: 
142:       filename = File.basename(URI.parse(src).path)
143:       filename << File.extname(hotlinked.path) unless filename["."]
144:       upload = UploadCreator.new(hotlinked, filename, origin: src).create_for(user_id)
145: 
146:       if upload.persisted?
147:         upload
148:       else
149:         log(
150:           :info,
151:           "Failed to persist downloaded hotlinked image for post: #{@post_id}: #{src} - #{upload.errors.full_messages.join("\n")}",
152:         )
153:         raise UploadCreateError
154:       end
155:     end
156: 
157:     def extract_images_from(html)
158:       doc = Nokogiri::HTML5.fragment(html)
159: 
160:       doc.css("img[src], [#{PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR}], a.lightbox[href]") -
161:         doc.css("img.avatar") - doc.css(".lightbox img[src]")
162:     end
163: 
164:     def should_download_image?(src, post = nil)
165:       # make sure we actually have a url
166:       return false if src.blank?
167: 
168:       local_bases =
169:         [Discourse.base_url, Discourse.asset_host, SiteSetting.external_emoji_url.presence].compact
170:           .map { |s| normalize_src(s) }
171: 
172:       if Discourse.store.has_been_uploaded?(src) || normalize_src(src).start_with?(*local_bases) ||
173:            src =~ %r{\A/[^/]}i
174:         return false if !(src =~ %r{/uploads/} || Upload.secure_uploads_url?(src))
175: 
176:         # Someone could hotlink a file from a different site on the same CDN,
177:         # so check whether we have it in this database
178:         #
179:         # if the upload already exists and is attached to a different post,
180:         # or the original_sha1 is missing meaning it was created before secure
181:         # media was enabled, then we definitely want to redownload again otherwise
182:         # we end up reusing existing uploads which may be linked to many posts
183:         # already.
184:         upload = Upload.consider_for_reuse(Upload.get_from_url(src), post)
185: 
186:         return !upload.present?
187:       end
188: 
189:       # Don't download non-local images unless site setting enabled
190:       return false unless SiteSetting.download_remote_images_to_local?
191: 
192:       # parse the src
193:       begin
194:         uri = URI.parse(src)
195:       rescue URI::Error
196:         return false
197:       end
198: 
199:       hostname = uri.hostname
200:       return false unless hostname
201: 
202:       # check the domains blocklist
203:       SiteSetting.should_download_images?(src)
204:     end
205: 
206:     def log(log_level, message)
207:       Rails.logger.public_send(
208:         log_level,
209:         "#{RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db}: #{message}",
210:       )
211:     end
212: 
213:     protected
214: 
215:     def replace_encoded_src(src)
216:       PostHotlinkedMedia.normalize_src(src, reset_scheme: false)
217:     end
218: 
219:     def normalize_src(src)
220:       PostHotlinkedMedia.normalize_src(src)
221:     end
222: 
223:     def disable_if_low_on_disk_space
224:       return if Discourse.store.external?
225:       return if !SiteSetting.download_remote_images_to_local
226:       return if available_disk_space >= SiteSetting.download_remote_images_threshold
227: 
228:       SiteSetting.download_remote_images_to_local = false
229: 
230:       # log the site setting change

232:       staff_action_logger = StaffActionLogger.new(Discourse.system_user)
233:       staff_action_logger.log_site_setting_change(
234:         "download_remote_images_to_local",
235:         true,
236:         false,
237:         details: reason,
238:       )
239: 
240:       # also send a private message to the site contact user notify_about_low_disk_space
241:       notify_about_low_disk_space
242:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: module Jobs
4:   # Sometimes we need to update the access control metadata for a _lot_ of objects on S3 (such as when secure uploads
5:   # is enabled), this is best spread out over many jobs instead of having to do the whole thing serially.
6:   class SyncAccessControlForUploads < ::Jobs::Base
7:     sidekiq_options queue: "low"
8: 
9:     def execute(args)
10:       return if !Discourse.store.external?
11:       return if !args.key?(:upload_ids)
12: 
13:       Upload
14:         .includes(:optimized_images)
15:         .where(id: args[:upload_ids])
16:         .find_in_batches do |uploads|
17:           uploads.each do |upload|
18:             begin
19:               Discourse.store.update_upload_access_control(upload, remove_existing_acl: true)
20:             rescue => err
21:               Discourse.warn_exception(
22:                 err,
23:                 message: "Failed to update upload access control",
24:                 env: {
25:                   upload_id: upload.id,
26:                   filename: upload.original_filename,
27:                 },
28:               )
29:             end
30:           end
31:         end
32:     end
33:   end
34: end

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Jobs
4:   class CleanUpUploads < ::Jobs::Scheduled
5:     every 1.hour
6: 
7:     def execute(args)
8:       grace_period = [SiteSetting.clean_orphan_uploads_grace_period_hours, 1].max
9: 
10:       # Always remove invalid upload records regardless of clean_up_uploads setting.
11:       Upload
12:         .by_users
13:         .where(
14:           "retain_hours IS NULL OR created_at < current_timestamp - interval '1 hour' * retain_hours",
15:         )
16:         .where("created_at < ?", grace_period.hour.ago)
17:         .where(url: "")
18:         .find_each(&:destroy!)
19: 
20:       return unless SiteSetting.clean_up_uploads?
21: 
22:       # Do nothing if the last cleanup was run too recently.
23:       last_cleanup_timestamp = last_cleanup
24:       if last_cleanup_timestamp.present? &&
25:            (Time.zone.now.to_i - last_cleanup_timestamp) < (grace_period / 2).hours
26:         return
27:       end
28: 
29:       result = Upload.by_users
30:       Upload.unused_callbacks&.each { |handler| result = handler.call(result) }
31: 
32:       # 1. Exclude uploads that have retain_hours set and are still within that retention period.
33:       # 2. Exclude uploads created in the grace period.
34:       # 3. Exclude secure uploads that have an access_control_post_id.
35:       # 4. Exclude uploads that are link to an upload reference.
36:       # 5. Exclude uploads that are linked to anything but a Post via UploadReference.
37:       result =
38:         result
39:           .where(
40:             "uploads.retain_hours IS NULL OR uploads.created_at < current_timestamp - interval '1 hour' * uploads.retain_hours",
41:           )
42:           .where("uploads.created_at < ?", grace_period.hour.ago)
43:           .where(
44:             "((uploads.access_control_post_id IS NULL) OR (uploads.access_control_post_id IS NOT NULL AND NOT uploads.secure))",
45:           )
46:           .joins("LEFT JOIN upload_references ON upload_references.upload_id = uploads.id")
47:           .where("upload_references.upload_id IS NULL")
48:           .with_no_non_post_relations
49: 
50:       result.find_each do |upload|
51:         next if Upload.in_use_callbacks&.any? { |callback| callback.call(upload) }
52:         upload.sha1.present? ? upload.destroy : upload.delete
53:       end
54: 
55:       ExternalUploadStub.cleanup!
56: 
57:       self.last_cleanup = Time.zone.now.to_i
58:     end
59: 
60:     def last_cleanup=(timestamp)
61:       Discourse.redis.setex(last_cleanup_key, 7.days.to_i, timestamp.to_s)
62:     end
63: 
64:     def last_cleanup
65:       timestamp = Discourse.redis.get(last_cleanup_key)
66:       timestamp ? timestamp.to_i : timestamp
67:     end
68: 
69:     def reset_last_cleanup!
70:       Discourse.redis.del(last_cleanup_key)
71:     end
72: 
73:     protected
74: 
75:     def last_cleanup_key
76:       "LAST_UPLOAD_CLEANUP"
77:     end
78:   end
79: end

(End of file - total 79 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Jobs
4:   class MigrateUploadScheme < ::Jobs::Scheduled
5:     every 10.minutes
6:     sidekiq_options retry: false
7: 
8:     def execute(args)
9:       return unless SiteSetting.migrate_to_new_scheme
10: 
11:       # clean up failed uploads
12:       Upload
13:         .where("created_at < ?", 1.hour.ago)
14:         .where("LENGTH(COALESCE(url, '')) = 0")
15:         .find_each { |upload| upload.destroy! }
16: 
17:       # migrate uploads to new scheme
18:       problems = Upload.migrate_to_new_scheme(limit: 50)
19: 
20:       problems.each do |hash|
21:         upload_id = hash[:upload].id
22:         Discourse.handle_job_exception(
23:           hash[:ex],
24:           error_context(args, "Migrating upload id #{upload_id}", upload_id: upload_id),
25:         )
26:       end
27: 
28:       # clean up failed optimized images
29:       OptimizedImage
30:         .where("LENGTH(COALESCE(url, '')) = 0")
31:         .find_each { |optimized_image| optimized_image.destroy! }
32: 
33:       # Clean up orphan optimized images
34:       OptimizedImage
35:         .joins("LEFT JOIN uploads ON optimized_images.upload_id = uploads.id")
36:         .where("uploads.id IS NULL")
37:         .find_each { |optimized_image| optimized_image.destroy! }
38: 
39:       # Clean up optimized images that needs to be regenerated
40:       OptimizedImage
41:         .joins(:upload)
42:         .where("optimized_images.url NOT LIKE '%/optimized/_X/%'")
43:         .where("uploads.url LIKE '%/original/_X/%'")
44:         .limit(50)
45:         .find_each do |optimized_image|
46:           upload = optimized_image.upload
47:           optimized_image.destroy!
48:           upload.rebake_posts_on_old_scheme
49:         end
50:     end
51:   end
52: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: module Jobs
3:   class CheckVideoConversionStatus < ::Jobs::Base
4:     sidekiq_options queue: "low", concurrency: 5
5: 
6:     def execute(args)
7:       return if args[:upload_id].blank? || args[:job_id].blank? || args[:adapter_type].blank?
8: 
9:       upload = Upload.find_by(id: args[:upload_id])
10:       return if upload.blank?
11: 
12:       adapter =
13:         VideoConversion::AdapterFactory.get_adapter(upload, adapter_type: args[:adapter_type])
14: 
15:       status = adapter.check_status(args[:job_id])
16: 
17:       case status
18:       when :complete
19:         if adapter.handle_completion(args[:job_id], args[:new_sha1])
20:           # Successfully completed
21:           Rails.logger.info(
22:             "Completed video conversion for upload ID #{upload.id} and job ID #{args[:job_id]}",
23:           )
24:         else
25:           # Handle completion failed
26:           Rails.logger.error(
27:             "Failed to handle video conversion completion for upload ID #{upload.id} and job ID #{args[:job_id]}",
28:           )
29:         end
30:       when :error
31:         Rails.logger.error(
32:           "Video conversion job failed for upload ID #{upload.id} and job ID #{args[:job_id]}",
33:         )
34:       when :pending
35:         # Re-enqueue the job to check again
36:         Jobs.enqueue_in(30.seconds, :check_video_conversion_status, args)
37:       end
38:     end
39:   end
40: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Jobs
4:   class PullUserProfileHotlinkedImages < ::Jobs::PullHotlinkedImages
5:     def execute(args)
6:       @user_id = args[:user_id]
7:       raise Discourse::InvalidParameters.new(:user_id) if @user_id.blank?
8: 
9:       user_profile = UserProfile.find_by(user_id: @user_id)
10:       return if user_profile.blank? || user_profile.bio_cooked.nil?
11: 
12:       large_image_urls = []
13:       broken_image_urls = []
14:       downloaded_images = {}
15: 
16:       extract_images_from(user_profile.bio_cooked).each do |node|
17:         download_src = original_src = node["src"] || node["href"]
18:         download_src =
19:           "#{SiteSetting.force_https ? "https" : "http"}:#{original_src}" if original_src.start_with?(
20:           "//",
21:         )
22:         normalized_src = normalize_src(download_src)
23: 
24:         next if !should_download_image?(download_src)
25: 
26:         begin
27:           already_attempted_download =
28:             downloaded_images.include?(normalized_src) ||
29:               large_image_urls.include?(normalized_src) ||
30:               broken_image_urls.include?(normalized_src)
31:           if !already_attempted_download
32:             downloaded_images[normalized_src] = attempt_download(download_src, @user_id)
33:           end
34:         rescue ImageTooLargeError
35:           large_image_urls << normalized_src
36:         rescue ImageBrokenError
37:           broken_image_urls << normalized_src
38:         end
39:       rescue => e
40:         raise e if Rails.env.test?
41:         log(
42:           :error,
43:           "Failed to pull hotlinked image (#{download_src}) user: #{@user_id}\n" + e.message +
44:             "\n" + e.backtrace.join("\n"),
45:         )
46:       end
47: 
48:       user_profile.bio_raw =
49:         InlineUploads.replace_hotlinked_image_urls(raw: user_profile.bio_raw) do |match_src|
50:           normalized_match_src = PostHotlinkedMedia.normalize_src(match_src)
51:           downloaded_images[normalized_match_src]
52:         end
53: 
54:       user_profile.skip_pull_hotlinked_image = true
55:       user_profile.save!
56:     end
57:   end
58: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: module Jobs
4:   class UpdateHotlinkedRaw < ::Jobs::Base
5:     sidekiq_options queue: "low"
6: 
7:     def execute(args)
8:       @post_id = args[:post_id]
9:       raise Discourse::InvalidParameters.new(:post_id) if @post_id.blank?
10: 
11:       post = Post.find_by(id: @post_id)
12:       return if post.nil?
13:       return if post.cook_method == Post.cook_methods[:raw_html]
14:       return if post.topic.nil?
15: 
16:       hotlinked_map = post.post_hotlinked_media.preload(:upload).index_by { |r| r.url }
17: 
18:       raw =
19:         InlineUploads.replace_hotlinked_image_urls(raw: post.raw) do |match_src|
20:           normalized_match_src = PostHotlinkedMedia.normalize_src(match_src)
21:           hotlinked_map[normalized_match_src]&.upload
22:         end
23: 
24:       if post.raw != raw
25:         changes = { raw: raw, edit_reason: I18n.t("upload.edit_reason") }
26:         post.revise(
27:           Discourse.system_user,
28:           changes,
29:           bypass_bump: true,
30:           skip_staff_log: true,
31:           skip_validations: true,
32:         )
33:       end
34:     end
35:   end
36: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # This model indicates an 'attempt' to create a topic thumbnail
4: # for an upload. This means we don't keep trying to create optimized
5: # images for small/invalid original images.
6: #
7: # Foreign keys with ON DELETE CASCADE are used to ensure unneeded data
8: # is deleted automatically
9: class TopicThumbnail < ActiveRecord::Base
10:   belongs_to :upload
11:   belongs_to :optimized_image
12: 
13:   def self.find_or_create_for!(original, max_width:, max_height:)
14:     existing =
15:       TopicThumbnail.find_by(upload: original, max_width: max_width, max_height: max_height)
16:     return existing if existing
17:     return nil if !SiteSetting.create_thumbnails?
18: 
19:     target_width, target_height =
20:       ImageSizer.resize(
21:         original.width,
22:         original.height,
23:         { max_width: max_width, max_height: max_height },
24:       )
25: 
26:     needs_optimization = target_width < original.width && target_height < original.height
27:     skip_animated = SiteSetting.animated_topic_thumbnails && original.animated?
28: 
29:     if needs_optimization && !skip_animated
30:       optimized = OptimizedImage.create_for(original, target_width, target_height)
31:     end
32: 
33:     # may have been associated already, bulk insert will skip dupes
34:     TopicThumbnail.insert_all(
35:       [
36:         upload_id: original.id,
37:         max_width: max_width,
38:         max_height: max_height,
39:         optimized_image_id: optimized&.id,
40:       ],
41:     )
42: 
43:     TopicThumbnail.find_by(upload: original, max_width: max_width, max_height: max_height)
44:   end
45: 
46:   def self.ensure_consistency!
47:     # Clean up records for broken upload links or broken optimized image links
48:     TopicThumbnail
49:       .joins("LEFT JOIN uploads on upload_id = uploads.id")
50:       .joins("LEFT JOIN optimized_images on optimized_image_id = optimized_images.id")
51:       .where(<<~SQL)
52:         (optimized_image_id IS NOT NULL AND optimized_images IS NULL)
53:         OR uploads IS NULL
54:       SQL
55:       .delete_all
56: 
57:     # Delete records for sizes which are no longer needed
58:     sizes =
59:       Topic.thumbnail_sizes +
60:         ThemeModifierHelper.new(theme_ids: Theme.pluck(:id)).topic_thumbnail_sizes
61:     sizes_sql =
62:       sizes.map { |s| "(max_width = #{s[0].to_i} AND max_height = #{s[1].to_i})" }.join(" OR ")
63:     TopicThumbnail.where.not(sizes_sql).delete_all
64:   end
65: end
66:

68: #
69: # Table name: topic_thumbnails
70: #
71: #  id                 :bigint           not null, primary key
72: #  upload_id          :bigint           not null
73: #  optimized_image_id :bigint
74: #  max_width          :integer          not null
75: #  max_height         :integer          not null
76: #
77: # Indexes

79: #  index_topic_thumbnails_on_optimized_image_id  (optimized_image_id)
80: #  index_topic_thumbnails_on_upload_id           (upload_id)
81: #  unique_topic_thumbnails                       (upload_id,max_width,max_height) UNIQUE

7:   has_many :upload_references, as: :target, dependent: :destroy
8:

10:     if saved_change_to_custom_upload_id? || saved_change_to_gravatar_upload_id?
11:       upload_ids = [self.custom_upload_id, self.gravatar_upload_id]
12:       UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: upload_ids, target: self)
13:     end
14:   end
15: 
16:   @@custom_user_gravatar_email_hash = {
17:     Discourse::SYSTEM_USER_ID => User.email_hash("info@discourse.org"),
18:   }
19: 
20:   def self.register_custom_user_gravatar_email_hash(user_id, email)
21:     @@custom_user_gravatar_email_hash[user_id] = User.email_hash(email)
22:   end
23: 
24:   def contains_upload?(id)
25:     gravatar_upload_id == id || custom_upload_id == id
26:   end
27: 
28:   def update_gravatar!
29:     DistributedMutex.synchronize("update_gravatar_#{user_id}") do
30:       begin
31:         self.update!(last_gravatar_download_attempt: Time.zone.now)
32: 
33:         max = Discourse.avatar_sizes.max
34: 
35:         # The user could be deleted before this executes
36:         return if user.blank? || user.primary_email.blank?
37: 
38:         email_hash = @@custom_user_gravatar_email_hash[user_id] || user.email_hash
39:         gravatar_url =
40:           "https://#{SiteSetting.gravatar_base_url}/avatar/#{email_hash}.png?s=#{max}&d=404&reset_cache=#{SecureRandom.urlsafe_base64(5)}"
41: 
42:         if SiteSetting.verbose_upload_logging
43:           Rails.logger.warn("Verbose Upload Logging: Downloading gravatar from #{gravatar_url}")
44:         end
45: 
46:         # follow redirects in case gravatar change rules on us
47:         tempfile =
48:           FileHelper.download(
49:             gravatar_url,

23: rescue StandardError
24:   false
25: end

27: def gather_uploads
28:   public_directory = "#{Rails.root}/public"
29:   current_db = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
30: 
31:   puts "", "Gathering uploads for '#{current_db}'...", ""
32: 
33:   Upload
34:     .where("url ~ '^\/uploads\/'")
35:     .where("url !~ ?", "^\/uploads\/#{current_db}")
36:     .find_each do |upload|
37:       begin
38:         old_db = upload.url[%r{\A/uploads/([^/]+)/}, 1]
39:         from = upload.url.dup
40:         to = upload.url.sub("/uploads/#{old_db}/", "/uploads/#{current_db}/")
41:         source = "#{public_directory}#{from}"
42:         destination = "#{public_directory}#{to}"
43: 
44:         # create destination directory & copy file unless it already exists
45:         unless file_exists?(destination)
46:           `mkdir -p '#{File.dirname(destination)}'`
47:           `cp --link '#{source}' '#{destination}'`
48:         end
49: 
50:         # ensure file has been successfully copied over
51:         raise unless file_exists?(destination)
52: 
53:         # remap links in db
54:         DbHelper.remap(from, to)
55:       rescue StandardError
56:         putc "!"
57:       else
58:         putc "."
59:       end
60:     end
61: 
62:   puts "", "Done!"
63: end

3: ##
4: # A note on determining whether an upload should be marked as secure:
5: #
6: # Some of these flags checked (e.g. all of the for_X flags and the opts[:type])
7: # are only set when _initially uploading_ via UploadCreator and are not present
8: # when an upload already exists, these will only be checked when the @creating
9: # option is present.
10: #
11: # If the upload already exists the best way to figure out whether it should be
12: # secure alongside the site settings is the access_control_post_id, because the
13: # original post the upload is linked to has far more bearing on its security context
14: # post-upload. If the access_control_post_id does not exist then we just rely
15: # on the current secure? status, otherwise there would be a lot of additional
16: # complex queries and joins to perform.
17: #
18: # These queries will be performed only if the @creating option is false. So if
19: # an upload is included in a post, and it's an upload from a different source
20: # (e.g. a category logo, site setting upload) then we will determine secure
21: # state _based on the first place the upload was referenced_.
22: #
23: # NOTE: When updating this to add more cases where uploads will be marked
24: # secure, consider uploads:secure_upload_analyse_and_update as well, which
25: # does not use this class directly but uses an SQL version of its rules for
26: # efficient updating of many uploads in bulk.
27: class UploadSecurity
28:   @@custom_public_types = []
29: 
30:   PUBLIC_TYPES = %w[
31:     avatar
32:     custom_emoji
33:     profile_background
34:     card_background
35:     category_logo
36:     category_logo_dark
37:     category_background
38:     group_flair
39:     badge_image
40:     site_setting
41:   ]
42: 
43:   PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES = %w[
44:     Badge
45:     Category
46:     CustomEmoji
47:     Group
48:     SiteSetting
49:     ThemeField
50:     User
51:     UserAvatar
52:     UserProfile
53:   ]
54: 
55:   def self.register_custom_public_type(type)
56:     @@custom_public_types << type if !@@custom_public_types.include?(type)
57:   end
58: 
59:   # used in tests
60:   def self.reset_custom_public_types
61:     @@custom_public_types = []
62:   end
63: 
64:   def initialize(upload, opts = {})
65:     @upload = upload
66:     @opts = opts
67:     @upload_type = @opts[:type]
68:     @creating = @opts[:creating]
69:   end
70: 
71:   def should_be_secure?
72:     should_be_secure_with_reason.first
73:   end
74: 
75:   def should_be_secure_with_reason
76:     insecure_context_checks.each { |check, reason| return false, reason if perform_check(check) }
77:     secure_context_checks.each do |check, reason|
78:       return perform_check(check), reason if priority_check?(check)
79:       return true, reason if perform_check(check)
80:     end

3: require "file_helper"
4: 
5: class UploadValidator < ActiveModel::Validator
6:   def validate(upload)
7:     # staff can upload any file in PM
8:     if (upload.for_private_message && SiteSetting.allow_staff_to_upload_any_file_in_pm)
9:       return true if upload.user&.staff?
10:     end
11: 
12:     # check the attachment blocklist
13:     if upload.for_group_message && SiteSetting.allow_all_attachments_for_group_messages
14:       return upload.original_filename =~ SiteSetting.blocked_attachment_filenames_regex
15:     end
16: 
17:     extension = File.extname(upload.original_filename)[1..-1] || ""
18: 
19:     return validate_site_setting_upload(upload, extension) if upload.for_site_setting
20: 
21:     if upload.for_gravatar && FileHelper.supported_gravatar_extensions.include?(extension)
22:       maximum_image_file_size(upload)
23:       return true
24:     end
25: 
26:     return true if changing_upload_security?(upload)
27: 
28:     if is_authorized?(upload, extension)
29:       if FileHelper.is_supported_image?(upload.original_filename)
30:         authorized_image_extension(upload, extension)
31:         maximum_image_file_size(upload)
32:       else
33:         authorized_attachment_extension(upload, extension)
34:         maximum_attachment_file_size(upload)
35:       end
36:     end
37:   end
38: 
39:   def validate_site_setting_upload(upload, extension)
40:     unless upload.user&.staff?
41:       upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.unauthorized"))
42:       return false
43:     end
44: 
45:     setting_opts = site_setting_type_hash(upload.site_setting_name)
46:     authorized_extensions = setting_opts[:authorized_extensions]
47: 
48:     if authorized_extensions.present?
49:       unless extension_allowed_for_site_setting?(extension, authorized_extensions)
50:         upload.errors.add(
51:           :original_filename,
52:           I18n.t("upload.unauthorized", authorized_extensions: authorized_extensions),
53:         )
54:         return false
55:       end
56: 
57:       validate_site_setting_file_size(upload, setting_opts)
58:     else
59:       unless FileHelper.is_supported_image?(upload.original_filename)
60:         upload.errors.add(:original_filename, I18n.t("upload.images_only"))
61:         return false
62:       end
63: 
64:       validate_site_setting_file_size(upload, setting_opts)
65:     end
66: 
67:     upload.errors.empty?
68:   end
69: 
70:   # this should only be run on existing records, and covers cases of
71:   # upload.update_secure_status being run outside of the creation flow,
72:   # where some cases e.g. have exemptions on the extension enforcement
73:   def changing_upload_security?(upload)
74:     !upload.new_record? &&
75:       upload.changed_attributes.keys.all? do |attribute|
76:         %w[secure security_last_changed_at security_last_changed_reason].include?(attribute)
77:       end
78:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: class UploadMarkdown
4:   def initialize(upload)
5:     @upload = upload
6:   end
7: 
8:   def to_markdown(display_name: nil)
9:     if FileHelper.is_supported_image?(@upload.original_filename)
10:       image_markdown(display_name: display_name)
11:     elsif FileHelper.is_supported_playable_media?(@upload.original_filename)
12:       playable_media_markdown(display_name: display_name)
13:     else
14:       attachment_markdown(display_name: display_name)
15:     end
16:   end
17: 
18:   def image_markdown(display_name: nil)
19:     display_name ||= @upload.original_filename
20:     "![#{self.class.escape_markdown(display_name)}|#{@upload.width}x#{@upload.height}](#{@upload.short_url})"
21:   end
22: 
23:   def attachment_markdown(display_name: nil, with_filesize: true)
24:     human_filesize = with_filesize ? " (#{@upload.human_filesize})" : ""
25:     display_name ||= @upload.original_filename
26: 
27:     "[#{self.class.escape_markdown(display_name)}|attachment](#{@upload.short_url})#{human_filesize}"
28:   end
29: 
30:   def playable_media_markdown(display_name: nil)
31:     type =
32:       if FileHelper.is_supported_audio?(@upload.original_filename)
33:         "audio"
34:       elsif FileHelper.is_supported_video?(@upload.original_filename)
35:         "video"
36:       end
37:     return attachment_markdown if !type
38:     display_name ||= @upload.original_filename
39:     "![#{self.class.escape_markdown(display_name)}|#{type}](#{@upload.short_url})"
40:   end
41: 
42:   def self.escape_markdown(text)
43:     text.gsub(/[\\*_~`\[\]|]/) { |c| "\\#{c}" }
44:   end
45: end

360:     def digest_posts
361:       Post.where(id: header_value("X-Discourse-Post-Ids")&.split(","))
362:     end
363: 
364:     def add_attachments(*posts, is_digest: false)
365:       max_email_size = SiteSetting.email_total_attachment_size_limit_kb.kilobytes
366:       return if max_email_size == 0
367: 
368:       email_size = 0
369:       posts.each do |post|
370:         next unless DiscoursePluginRegistry.apply_modifier(:should_add_email_attachments, post)
371: 
372:         post.uploads.each do |original_upload|
373:           optimized_1X = original_upload.optimized_images.first
374: 
375:           if FileHelper.is_supported_image?(original_upload.original_filename)
376:             next if !should_attach_image?(original_upload, optimized_1X)
377:             # Don't attach images that aren't rendered in the e-mail.
378:             next if is_digest && !@message.html_part.body.include?(original_upload.sha1)
379:           else
380:             # Only attach images in digests.
381:             next if is_digest
382:           end
383: 
384:           attached_upload = optimized_1X || original_upload
385:           next if email_size + attached_upload.filesize > max_email_size
386: 
387:           begin
388:             path =
389:               if attached_upload.local?
390:                 Discourse.store.path_for(attached_upload)
391:               else
392:                 Discourse.store.download!(attached_upload)
393:               end
394: 
395:             @message_attachments_index[original_upload.sha1] = @message.attachments.size
396:             @message.attachments[original_upload.original_filename] = File.read(path)
397:             email_size += File.size(path)
398:           rescue => e
399:             Discourse.warn_exception(
400:               e,
401:               message: "Failed to attach file to email",
402:               env: {
403:                 post_id: post.id,
404:                 upload_id: original_upload.id,
405:                 filename: original_upload.original_filename,
406:               },
407:             )
408:           end
409:         end
410:       end
411: 
412:       fix_parts_after_attachments!
413:     end
414: 
415:     def should_attach_image?(upload, optimized_1X = nil)
416:       if !SiteSetting.secure_uploads_allow_embed_images_in_emails ||
417:            # Sometimes images in a post have a secure URL but are not secure uploads,
418:            # for example if a user uploads an image to a public post then copies the markdown
419:            # into a PM which sends an email, so we have to make sure we attached those
420:            # stripped images here as well.
421:            (
422:              !upload.secure? && !@stripped_secure_upload_shas.include?(upload.sha1) &&
423:                !@stripped_secure_upload_shas.include?(optimized_1X&.sha1)
424:            )
425:         return
426:       end
427:       if (optimized_1X&.filesize || upload.filesize) >
428:            SiteSetting.secure_uploads_max_email_embed_image_size_kb.kilobytes
429:         return
430:       end
431:       true
432:     end
433: 
434:     #
435:     # Two behaviors in the mail gem collide:
436:     #
437:     #  1. Attachments are added as extra parts at the top level,
438:     #  2. When there are both text and html parts, the content type is set
439:     #     to 'multipart/alternative'.

40:   #   - :value [String] the value of the setting

43:     sql = <<~SQL

45:         tss.name AS setting_name, tss.value, tss.data_type
46:       FROM themes theme

48:       WHERE theme.component = false AND tss.name IN (:setting_names)
